message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user (Sean) is building FRAME//LOG, a native Android film photography shot-logging app. The session had three main implementation requests:
   1. Run `./gradlew compileDebugKotlin` and fix all compile errors before adding new screens (UI files from the previous session were unverified)
   2. Implement `RollListScreen.kt` following the Roll List spec in `docs/Screen Inventory/FRAMELOG screen inventory.md` and the wireframe in `docs/Wireframes/WIREFRAME_roll_list_v2.svg`
   3. Implement `RollJournalScreen.kt` following the Roll Journal spec and wireframe (`docs/Wireframes/WIREFRAME_roll_journal_v2.svg`) — this was in progress when the summary was requested

   The user also asked to save a session note to basic-memory and recalled project memory at the start of the session.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Jetpack Compose** with **Material 3** (BOM 2024.09.00 → Material3 1.3.0)
   - **Room ORM** with `@Embedded`, `@Relation`, `@Transaction`, `@Junction`, `@Query` JOIN with correlated subqueries
   - **Navigation Compose 2.8.0** with type-safe `@Serializable` routes
   - **AndroidViewModel** + `SavedStateHandle` for screen ViewModels
   - **StateFlow** + **Channel** for unidirectional data flow (UI state + one-shot events)
   - **SwipeToDismissBox** (Material3 experimental) with `confirmValueChange = { false }` snap-back pattern for swipe-to-load
   - **combinedClickable** (`ExperimentalFoundationApi`) for tap + long-press on cards
   - **DropdownMenu** for context menus anchored inside a `Box`
   - **ModalBottomSheet** (Material3) for the export format picker
   - **`drawBehind` + `PathEffect.dashPathEffect`** for dashed border Modifier extension
   - **Material Icons core set** constraint — `ContentCopy`, `Remove`, `CameraAlt` not available without `material-icons-extended`
   - **`@Embedded` JOIN result** pattern (`RollListRow`) for enriched list queries without loading full `RollWithDetails`
   - **`ExposureValues`** utility: `shutterDisplayValue()`, `isLongExposure()`, `formatExposureCompensation()`
   - **`AppPreferences`** SharedPreferences wrapper: `activeRollId`, `getCurrentFrameNumber(rollId)`, `ExportFormat`, `AppTheme`
   - **Export flow**: `ExportFormatter` (CSV/JSON/plain text) → Android `Intent.ACTION_SEND` share sheet
   - **JAVA_HOME** must be set to Android Studio JBR: `C:/Program Files/Android/Android Studio/jbr`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`C:\Users\flatl\.claude\projects\E--AndroidProjects-FrameLog\memory\project_framelog_status.md`**
     - Auto-memory file tracking full project implementation status
     - Updated twice in this session to reflect completed Gear Library screens and RollListScreen

   - **`app/.../ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt`** (CREATED)
     - Full form: name, make, model, `MountTypeField` (autocomplete), format dropdown (MEDIUM_FORMAT excluded), shutter increments dropdown
     - Defines `internal val CameraBodyFormat.label` and `internal val ShutterIncrements.label`
     - Same structure as `LensDetailScreen.kt`: Scaffold + TopAppBar + scrollable Column + DiscardDialog + DeleteDialog

   - **`app/.../ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt`** (CREATED)
     - Full form: name, make, `FilterTypeField` (private autocomplete composable matching `MountTypeField` pattern), EV reduction (optional decimal), filter size (optional int), notes
     - `FilterTypeField` is private (only used in this file, unlike `MountTypeField` which is internal/shared)

   - **`app/.../ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt`** (CREATED)
     - Full form: name, make, ISO, format dropdown (MEDIUM_FORMAT excluded), default frame count, color type dropdown, discontinued `Switch` with description, notes
     - Defines `internal val FilmFormat.label`
     - Reuses `ColorType.label` from `GearLibraryScreen.kt` (declared `internal` there)
     - Includes comment: `// ColorType.label is defined in GearLibraryScreen.kt (internal, same package)`

   - **`app/.../ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt`** (CREATED)
     - Complex form: kit name, `CameraBodyPickerField` (private dropdown handling null selection), `AddLensDropdown` (mount-type filtered, disabled when no body selected), lens list with `RadioButton` for primary + `Icons.Default.Clear` remove button, `FilterToggleRow` (checkbox per available filter), notes
     - `SnackbarHostState` for `IncompatibleLensesRemoved` event
     - Duplicate action replaced with `TextButton("Duplicate")` (not an icon)

   - **`app/.../ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt`** (MODIFIED)
     - Changed `private val ColorType.label` → `internal val ColorType.label` to make it accessible from `FilmStockDetailScreen.kt`

   - **`app/.../data/db/relation/RollListRow.kt`** (CREATED)
     ```kotlin
     data class RollListRow(
         @Embedded val roll: Roll,
         val filmStockName: String,
         val filmStockMake: String,
         val cameraBodyName: String,
         val cameraBodyMake: String,
         /** Count of frames where isLogged = true. Derived via subquery. */
         val loggedFrameCount: Int,
     )
     ```
     - Required because `searchRollsByStatus()` returns `List<Roll>` which lacks display names and frame counts needed by the card UI

   - **`app/.../data/db/dao/RollDao.kt`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added import for `RollListRow`
     - Added new query method:
     ```kotlin
     @Query("""
         SELECT r.id, r.name, r.filmStockId, r.cameraBodyId, r.pushPull, r.ratedISO,
                r.filmExpiryDate, r.totalExposures, r.isLoaded, r.gpsEnabled, r.status,
                r.loadedAt, r.finishedAt, r.lastExportedAt, r.notes,
                fs.name AS filmStockName, fs.make AS filmStockMake,
                cb.name AS cameraBodyName, cb.make AS cameraBodyMake,
                (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM frames f WHERE f.rollId = r.id AND f.isLogged = 1) AS loggedFrameCount
         FROM rolls r
         JOIN film_stocks fs ON r.filmStockId = fs.id
         JOIN camera_bodies cb ON r.cameraBodyId = cb.id
         WHERE r.name LIKE '%' || :query || '%' AND r.status = :status
         ORDER BY r.loadedAt DESC
     """)
     abstract fun searchRollListRowsByStatus(query: String, status: String): Flow<List<RollListRow>>
     ```

   - **`app/.../data/repository/RollRepository.kt`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added import for `RollListRow`
     - Added: `fun searchRollListRowsByStatus(query: String, status: String): Flow<List<RollListRow>> = rollDao.searchRollListRowsByStatus(query, status)`

   - **`app/.../ui/rolls/RollListViewModel.kt`** (REPLACED)
     - Changed `UiState` fields from `List<Roll>` to `List<RollListRow>`
     - Renamed `onLoadRollSwipedOrLongPressed()` → `onLoadRollRequested()`
     - Renamed `onArchiveTapped()` → `onArchiveConfirmed()` and `onUnarchiveTapped()` → `onUnarchiveConfirmed()` (since confirmation is shown in UI before calling VM)
     - Three `collectXxxRolls()` methods updated to call `searchRollListRowsByStatus()`

   - **`app/.../ui/rolls/RollListScreen.kt`** (CREATED)
     - Full screen with `TabRow` (3 tabs: Active with count, Finished with count if >0, Archived with count if >0)
     - `OutlinedTextField` search bar with `Icons.Default.Search` leading icon and `Icons.Default.Clear` trailing icon
     - `when(state.selectedTab)` dispatch to `ActiveTabContent`, `FinishedTabContent`, `ArchivedTabContent`
     - `ActiveTabContent`: splits `activeRolls` into loaded/unloaded, shows "In camera" and "Inventory" section labels
     - `SwipeableRollCard` wrapper using `SwipeToDismissBox` with `confirmValueChange = { value -> if (value != Settled) onSwipedToLoad(); false }` — always snaps back
     - Swipe background shows "⟵  load roll  ⟶" text in `secondaryContainer`
     - `RollCard` composable (single composable for all roll types):
       - `@OptIn(ExperimentalFoundationApi::class)` + `combinedClickable(onClick, onLongClick)`
       - Visual: loaded = solid border (`border(0.5.dp)`), unloaded = `dashedBorder()` custom modifier
       - Leading 32dp Box: `primaryContainer` fill for loaded, `surfaceVariant` for unloaded (placeholder for future camera/canister icons)
       - Muted text (`textAlpha = 0.55f`) for unloaded and archived
       - Progress bar: `LinearProgressIndicator(progress = { loggedFrameCount.toFloat() / totalExposures })`
       - Swipe hint "← swipe to load →" for unloaded cards
       - Notes preview (first line, italic) if non-null
       - `DropdownMenu` (state-aware): Load Roll (unloaded active only), Open Journal, Archive (finished only), Unarchive (archived only), Delete (always, error color)
     - 4 `AlertDialog` confirmations: Load, Delete, Archive, Unarchive
     - `EmptyRollState` composable for empty tabs
     - `SectionLabel` composable (UPPERCASE, `labelSmall`, `onSurfaceVariant`)
     - `dashedBorder()` private Modifier extension:
       ```kotlin
       private fun Modifier.dashedBorder(color: Color, cornerRadius: Dp): Modifier =
           this.drawBehind {
               drawRoundRect(
                   color = color,
                   style = Stroke(width = 1.dp.toPx(), pathEffect = PathEffect.dashPathEffect(floatArrayOf(8f, 5f), 0f)),
                   cornerRadius = CornerRadius(cornerRadius.toPx()),
               )
           }
       ```
     - `dateFormatter = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd", Locale.getDefault())`

   - **`app/.../ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.rolls.RollListScreen`
     - Uncommented and wired `composable<RollList> { RollListScreen(navController = navController) }`
     - Roll Setup, Kit Selector, Roll Journal, Frame Detail remain commented with `— TODO next session`

   - **Reference files read for RollJournal (not yet written):**
     - `WIREFRAME_roll_journal_v2.svg`: TopAppBar (← name ⋯), subtitle (filmStock · loaded state · ISO), progress bar row, search/sort row, frame list (logged=solid+filled circle, unlogged=dashed+empty circle), bottom bar (unloaded: "load roll" + "export ▾"; loaded: "finish roll" + "export ▾"; finished/archived: "export ▾" only), Load confirmation sheet, Delete confirmation sheet
     - `RollJournalViewModel.kt`: `RollJournalUiState` (roll: RollWithDetails?, currentFrameNumber, showExportSheet, selectedExportFormat, isLoading, isActionInProgress); Events: NavigateToFrameDetail, ShowFinishRollConfirmation, ShowLoadRollConfirmation, ShowArchiveConfirmation, ShowUnarchiveConfirmation, ShowDeleteConfirmation, ShareExportContent(content, mimeType, suggestedFilename), NavigateBack, ShowErrorMessage
     - `FrameWithDetails.kt`: `@Embedded Frame` + `Lens?` (via lensId) + `List<Filter>` (via Junction on FrameFilter)
     - `AppPreferences.kt`: `ExportFormat` enum (CSV, JSON, PLAIN_TEXT) defined here; `getCurrentFrameNumber(rollId): Int`
     - `RollLensWithLens.kt`: `@Embedded RollLens` (has `lensId` field) + `Lens` @Relation
     - `RollFilterWithFilter.kt`: `@Embedded RollFilter` + `Filter` @Relation
     - `ExposureValues.kt`: `shutterDisplayValue(stored)`, `isLongExposure(stored)`, `formatExposureCompensation(value)`

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **JAVA_HOME not set**: `./gradlew` failed with "JAVA_HOME is not set". Fixed by prefixing all Gradle commands with `JAVA_HOME="C:/Program Files/Android/Android Studio/jbr"`
   - **4 unresolved references in NavGraph.kt**: `CameraBodyDetailScreen`, `FilmStockDetailScreen`, `FilterDetailScreen`, `KitDetailScreen` didn't exist. Fixed by creating all four screen files.
   - **`ColorType.label` overload resolution ambiguity**: `GearLibraryScreen.kt` had `private val ColorType.label` and `FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` independently defined the same extension. Even though `private` makes it file-scoped, the compiler saw both during compilation of `GearLibraryScreen.kt`. Fixed by: (1) changing `private` to `internal` in `GearLibraryScreen.kt`, (2) removing the duplicate from `FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` and adding a comment pointing to the shared definition.
   - **`Icons.Default.ContentCopy` and `Icons.Default.Remove` unresolved**: These icons are in `material-icons-extended`, not the core icon set. Fixed by replacing `ContentCopy` icon with `TextButton("Duplicate")` and `Remove` icon with `Icons.Default.Clear` (which IS in core).

5. Problem Solving:
   - **`List<Roll>` insufficient for card display**: The existing `searchRollsByStatus()` DAO method returned plain `Roll` objects which only have `filmStockId` and `cameraBodyId` (foreign keys), not the display names needed for roll cards. Solved by creating `RollListRow` with `@Embedded Roll` + JOIN fields + correlated subquery for logged frame count, then adding a new DAO method, repository method, and updating the ViewModel.
   - **Material Icons core set limitation**: The project uses `material3` without `material-icons-extended`. Consistently worked around by using core icons or text buttons instead of extended icons. Documented need to eventually add `material-icons-extended` for proper camera/canister icons on roll cards.
   - **Swipe-to-load card behavior**: `SwipeToDismissBox` defaults to dismissing items. Required using `confirmValueChange = { value -> if (value != Settled) triggerAction(); false }` to trigger the load action while always snapping the card back to its original position.
   - **Per-frame filter data unavailable in RollJournal**: `RollWithDetails.frames` is `List<Frame>` (plain), not `List<FrameWithDetails>`. Changing the type would require data model changes and risk breaking compiled code. Decision: omit filter display from journal frame cards; full filter data available in frame detail screen.

6. All User Messages:
   - "Recall FrameLog project memory and summarize what was done yesterday please."
   - "Recall FrameLog project memory, then run ./gradlew compileDebugKotlin to check if the UI files from last session compile cleanly. Fix any errors before adding new screens."
   - "Save to basic-memory: FrameLog session 2026-03-20 morning. Gear Library UI complete — all 5 list screens and 5 detail screens building cleanly. NavRoutes.kt and NavGraph.kt in place. JAVA_HOME needs to be set permanently in Windows environment variables. Next session: Roll screens — RollListScreen, RollSetupScreen, RollJournalScreen, FrameDetailScreen. Start with RollListScreen."
   - "Implement RollListScreen.kt following the Roll List spec in docs/FRAMELOG_screen_inventory.md and the wireframe in docs/Wireframes/WIREFRAME_roll_list_v2.svg."
   - "Implement RollJournalScreen.kt following the Roll Journal spec in docs/FRAMELOG_screen_inventory.md and the wireframe in docs/Wireframes/WIREFRAME_roll_journal_v2.svg."
   - [Summary request - current message]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Implement `RollJournalScreen.kt`** — explicitly requested, all reference material read, implementation not yet started
   - **Implement `RollSetupScreen.kt`** — mentioned in session notes as next after Roll List
   - **Implement `FrameDetailScreen.kt`** — mentioned in session notes
   - Wire `RollJournal`, `FrameDetail`, `RollSetup`, `KitSelector` routes in `NavGraph.kt` (currently commented as TODO)
   - Implement `QuickScreen`, `SettingsScreen`, Onboarding screens
   - Consider adding `material-icons-extended` dependency for proper camera/canister icons on roll cards

8. Current Work:
   The work immediately before this summary request was gathering all reference material needed to implement `RollJournalScreen.kt`. All reading was complete and the implementation was about to begin. Key information gathered:

   **From `WIREFRAME_roll_journal_v2.svg`:**
   - TopAppBar: back arrow (←), roll name (center), overflow menu (⋯)
   - Subtitle row: "Kodak Double-X · not loaded · ISO 250"
   - Progress bar: `[████░░░░] 0 / 38 logged`
   - Search + sort row: "search frames..." + "sort: frame ▾"
   - Frame cards: unlogged = dashed border + empty circle + "— unlogged —"; logged = solid border + filled circle + exposure data
   - Bottom bar varies by state: unloaded → "load roll" + "export ▾"; loaded → "finish roll" + "export ▾"; finished/archived → "export ▾" only
   - Load roll confirmation sheet and delete confirmation sheet shown in wireframe

   **From `RollJournalViewModel.kt`:**
   - State: `roll: RollWithDetails?`, `currentFrameNumber: Int`, `showExportSheet: Boolean`, `selectedExportFormat: ExportFormat`, `isLoading: Boolean`, `isActionInProgress: Boolean`
   - Events: NavigateToFrameDetail(frameId, rollId), ShowFinishRollConfirmation, ShowLoadRollConfirmation, ShowArchiveConfirmation, ShowUnarchiveConfirmation, ShowDeleteConfirmation, ShareExportContent(content, mimeType, suggestedFilename), NavigateBack, ShowErrorMessage
   - VM methods: `onFrameCardTapped()`, `onExportTapped()`, `onExportFormatSelected()`, `onExportDismissed()`, `onExportConfirmed()`, `onLoadRollTapped()`, `onLoadRollConfirmed()`, `onFinishRollTapped()`, `onFinishRollConfirmed()`, `onArchiveTapped()`, `onArchiveConfirmed()`, `onUnarchiveTapped()`, `onUnarchiveConfirmed()`, `onDeleteTapped()`, `onDeleteConfirmed()`

   **Key constraints identified:**
   - `RollWithDetails.frames` is `List<Frame>` (NOT `List<FrameWithDetails>`), so per-frame filter data is unavailable → filters will be omitted from journal cards
   - Lens name lookup: build `Map<Int, Lens>` from `rollWithDetails.lenses.associate { it.rollLens.lensId to it.lens }`
   - Shutter display: use `ExposureValues.shutterDisplayValue(stored)` and `ExposureValues.isLongExposure(stored)` for accent color
   - EC display: use `ExposureValues.formatExposureCompensation(value)`
   - Share export: `context.startActivity(Intent.createChooser(Intent(ACTION_SEND).apply { type = mimeType; putExtra(EXTRA_TEXT, content) }, null))`
   - `ExportFormat` enum is defined in `AppPreferences.kt` (not a separate file)
   - `Icons.Default.MoreVert` needed for overflow menu (should be in core icon set)

9. Optional Next Step:
   Implement `RollJournalScreen.kt` as explicitly requested: "Implement RollJournalScreen.kt following the Roll Journal spec in docs/FRAMELOG_screen_inventory.md and the wireframe in docs/Wireframes/WIREFRAME_roll_journal_v2.svg."

   The screen needs:
   - `Scaffold` with `TopAppBar` (back, roll name, overflow ⋯ menu with Archive/Unarchive/Delete), state-dependent `bottomBar`, `SnackbarHost`
   - Fixed header below AppBar: subtitle line (filmStock · loaded status · ISO) + progress bar
   - Search + sort row (local state: `frameQuery`, `FrameSort` enum ASCENDING/DESCENDING)
   - `LazyColumn` of frame cards: logged (solid border, filled circle, aperture/shutter/lens/EC/GPS/notes) and unlogged (dashed border, empty circle, "— unlogged —"); current frame gets accent border + "CURRENT" label
   - Bottom bar: unloaded active → "Load Roll" + "Export"; loaded active → "Finish Roll" + "Export"; finished/archived → "Export" only
   - `ModalBottomSheet` for export format picker (CSV/JSON/Plain Text radio buttons + Export button)
   - 5 `AlertDialog` confirmations: Load Roll, Finish Roll (irreversible warning), Archive, Unarchive, Delete (danger)
   - `LaunchedEffect` for events including Android share intent for `ShareExportContent`
   - Wire `RollJournal` route in `NavGraph.kt`

1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.rolls
     2→
     3→import android.app.Application
     4→import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
     5→import androidx.lifecycle.SavedStateHandle
     6→import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
     7→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.AppPreferences
     8→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.ExportFormat
     9→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.AppDatabase
    10→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.relation.RollWithDetails
    11→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.repository.RollRepository
    12→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.util.ExportFormatter
    13→import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.Channel
    14→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
    15→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
    16→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
    18→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.receiveAsFlow
    19→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
    20→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
    21→
    22→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→// UiState
    24→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25→
    26→data class RollJournalUiState(
    27→    val roll: RollWithDetails? = null,
    28→    /** The "current" frame number — the frame the Quick Screen is pointing at. */
    29→    val currentFrameNumber: Int = 1,
    30→    val showExportSheet: Boolean = false,
    31→    val selectedExportFormat: ExportFormat = ExportFormat.CSV,
    32→    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
    33→    val isActionInProgress: Boolean = false,
    34→)
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// Events
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→sealed class RollJournalEvent {
    41→    data class NavigateToFrameDetail(val frameId: Int, val rollId: Int) : RollJournalEvent()
    42→    /** Emit to show the Finish Roll confirmation sheet. */
    43→    data object ShowFinishRollConfirmation : RollJournalEvent()
    44→    /** Emit to show the Load Roll confirmation sheet. */
    45→    data object ShowLoadRollConfirmation : RollJournalEvent()
    46→    /** Emit to show the Archive confirmation sheet. */
    47→    data object ShowArchiveConfirmation : RollJournalEvent()
    48→    /** Emit to show the Unarchive confirmation sheet. */
    49→    data object ShowUnarchiveConfirmation : RollJournalEvent()
    50→    /** Emit to show the delete danger confirmation. */
    51→    data object ShowDeleteConfirmation : RollJournalEvent()
    52→    /**
    53→     * Emit to hand the formatted export content to the Android share sheet.
    54→     * The UI creates the Intent; ViewModel provides the content and MIME type.
    55→     */
    56→    data class ShareExportContent(
    57→        val content: String,
    58→        val mimeType: String,
    59→        val suggestedFilename: String,
    60→    ) : RollJournalEvent()
    61→    data object NavigateBack : RollJournalEvent()
    62→    data class ShowErrorMessage(val message: String) : RollJournalEvent()
    63→}
    64→
    65→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→// ViewModel
    67→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68→
    69→class RollJournalViewModel(
    70→    application: Application,
    71→    savedState: SavedStateHandle,
    72→) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    73→
    74→    private val rollId: Int = checkNotNull(savedState["rollId"]) {
    75→        "RollJournalViewModel requires a 'rollId' nav argument"
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    private val db = AppDatabase.getInstance(application)
    79→    private val rollRepository = RollRepository(db)
    80→    private val appPreferences = AppPreferences(application)
    81→
    82→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(RollJournalUiState())
    83→    val state: StateFlow<RollJournalUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    84→
    85→    private val _events = Channel<RollJournalEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    86→    val events: Flow<RollJournalEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    87→
    88→    init {
    89→        viewModelScope.launch {
    90→            rollRepository.getRollById(rollId).collect { rollWithDetails ->
    91→                val currentFrame = appPreferences.getCurrentFrameNumber(rollId)
    92→                _state.update {
    93→                    it.copy(
    94→                        roll = rollWithDetails,
    95→                        currentFrameNumber = currentFrame,
    96→                        isLoading = false,
    97→                    )
    98→                }
    99→            }
   100→        }
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104→    // User actions
   105→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   106→
   107→    fun onFrameCardTapped(frameId: Int) = viewModelScope.launch {
   108→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.NavigateToFrameDetail(frameId, rollId))
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    fun onExportTapped() {
   112→        _state.update {
   113→            it.copy(
   114→                showExportSheet = true,
   115→                selectedExportFormat = appPreferences.defaultExportFormat,
   116→            )
   117→        }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    fun onExportFormatSelected(format: ExportFormat) {
   121→        _state.update { it.copy(selectedExportFormat = format) }
   122→    }
   123→
   124→    fun onExportDismissed() {
   125→        _state.update { it.copy(showExportSheet = false) }
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    fun onExportConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   129→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = true, showExportSheet = false) }
   130→        try {
   131→            val export = rollRepository.getRollForExport(rollId)
   132→            val rollName = _state.value.roll?.roll?.name ?: "roll_$rollId"
   133→            val format = _state.value.selectedExportFormat
   134→
   135→            val (content, mimeType, extension) = when (format) {
   136→                ExportFormat.CSV -> Triple(
   137→                    ExportFormatter.toCsv(export),
   138→                    "text/csv",
   139→                    "csv",
   140→                )
   141→                ExportFormat.JSON -> Triple(
   142→                    ExportFormatter.toJson(export),
   143→                    "application/json",
   144→                    "json",
   145→                )
   146→                ExportFormat.PLAIN_TEXT -> Triple(
   147→                    ExportFormatter.toPlainText(export),
   148→                    "text/plain",
   149→                    "txt",
   150→                )
   151→            }
   152→
   153→            val filename = sanitizeFilename(rollName) + ".$extension"
   154→            rollRepository.updateLastExported(rollId, System.currentTimeMillis())
   155→
   156→            _events.send(
   157→                RollJournalEvent.ShareExportContent(
   158→                    content = content,
   159→                    mimeType = mimeType,
   160→                    suggestedFilename = filename,
   161→                )
   162→            )
   163→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   164→            _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowErrorMessage("Export failed: ${e.message}"))
   165→        } finally {
   166→            _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = false) }
   167→        }
   168→    }
   169→
   170→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   171→    // Roll state transitions (confirmation is handled by the UI before calling these)
   172→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→
   174→    fun onLoadRollTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   175→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowLoadRollConfirmation)
   176→    }
   177→
   178→    fun onLoadRollConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   179→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = true) }
   180→        rollRepository.updateIsLoaded(rollId, true)
   181→        if (appPreferences.activeRollId == -1) {
   182→            appPreferences.activeRollId = rollId
   183→        }
   184→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = false) }
   185→    }
   186→
   187→    fun onFinishRollTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   188→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowFinishRollConfirmation)
   189→    }
   190→
   191→    fun onFinishRollConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   192→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = true) }
   193→        rollRepository.finishRoll(rollId, System.currentTimeMillis())
   194→        // Unload the roll — a finished roll is no longer in a camera
   195→        rollRepository.updateIsLoaded(rollId, false)
   196→        // If this was the active roll, clear the selection
   197→        if (appPreferences.activeRollId == rollId) {
   198→            appPreferences.activeRollId = -1
   199→        }
   200→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = false) }
   201→    }
   202→
   203→    fun onArchiveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   204→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowArchiveConfirmation)
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    fun onArchiveConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   208→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = true) }
   209→        rollRepository.archiveRoll(rollId)
   210→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = false) }
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    fun onUnarchiveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   214→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowUnarchiveConfirmation)
   215→    }
   216→
   217→    fun onUnarchiveConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   218→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = true) }
   219→        rollRepository.unarchiveRoll(rollId)
   220→        _state.update { it.copy(isActionInProgress = false) }
   221→    }
   222→
   223→    fun onDeleteTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   224→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.ShowDeleteConfirmation)
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    fun onDeleteConfirmed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   228→        val roll = _state.value.roll?.roll ?: return@launch
   229→        appPreferences.clearRollPreferences(rollId)
   230→        if (appPreferences.activeRollId == rollId) {
   231→            appPreferences.activeRollId = -1
   232→        }
   233→        rollRepository.deleteRoll(roll)
   234→        _events.send(RollJournalEvent.NavigateBack)
   235→    }
   236→
   237→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   238→    // Helpers
   239→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   240→
   241→    private fun sanitizeFilename(name: String): String =
   242→        name.replace(Regex("[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]"), "_")
   243→}

package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity

import androidx.room.Entity
import androidx.room.ForeignKey
import androidx.room.Index
import androidx.room.PrimaryKey

/**
 * A single exposure slot on a roll.
 *
 * All frames are pre-created in bulk when a roll is loaded — one for each of
 * Roll.totalExposures. An unlogged frame has isLogged = false and null exposure fields.
 *
 * [frameNumber] is immutable. Set at roll creation, never changes. This is the canonical
 * ordering key for frames.
 *
 * [isLogged] and [loggedAt] are deliberately decoupled: a frame can be marked as logged
 * retroactively without fabricating a timestamp. isLogged = true with loggedAt = null is
 * valid (retroactive log). isLogged = false with loggedAt = null is an unlogged slot.
 *
 * [aperture] and [shutterSpeed] are stored as canonical strings (e.g. "f/5.6", "1/125").
 * Valid values are enforced by the UI steppers, not the database.
 *
 * Active filters are in the FrameFilter join table, not here. Filters stack.
 */
@Entity(
    tableName = "frames",
    foreignKeys = [
        ForeignKey(
            entity = Roll::class,
            parentColumns = ["id"],
            childColumns = ["rollId"],
            onDelete = ForeignKey.CASCADE,
        ),
        ForeignKey(
            entity = Lens::class,
            parentColumns = ["id"],
            childColumns = ["lensId"],
        ),
    ],
    indices = [Index("rollId"), Index("lensId")],
)
data class Frame(
    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true) val id: Int = 0,
    val rollId: Int,
    /** Immutable — 1 through Roll.totalExposures, set at roll creation. */
    val frameNumber: Int,
    val isLogged: Boolean = false,
    /** When the frame was logged, as epoch milliseconds. Null if never logged or logged retroactively. */
    val loggedAt: Long? = null,
    /** Canonical string, e.g. "f/5.6". Null if unlogged. */
    val aperture: String? = null,
    /** Canonical string, e.g. "1/125", "1s", "B". Null if unlogged. */
    val shutterSpeed: String? = null,
    /** Must reference a lens in this roll's RollLens table. Enforced at application layer. */
    val lensId: Int? = null,
    /** Exposure compensation in EV stops, e.g. -1.0, +0.5. Null if not applied. */
    val exposureCompensation: Float? = null,
    /** GPS latitude. Captured automatically if Roll.gpsEnabled = true. */
    val lat: Double? = null,
    /** GPS longitude. Captured automatically if Roll.gpsEnabled = true. */
    val lng: Double? = null,
    val notes: String? = null,
)

1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data
     2→
     3→import android.content.Context
     4→import androidx.core.content.edit
     5→
     6→private const val PREFS_NAME = "framelog_prefs"
     7→
     8→// Global keys
     9→private const val KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE = "onboarding_complete"
    10→private const val KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID = "active_roll_id"
    11→private const val KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL = "extra_frames_per_roll"
    12→private const val KEY_GPS_PRECISION = "gps_precision"
    13→private const val KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT = "default_export_format"
    14→private const val KEY_APP_THEME = "app_theme"
    15→private const val KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE = "accessible_color_mode"
    16→private const val KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN = "tip_jar_prompt_shown"
    17→
    18→// Per-roll keys
    19→private fun keyCurrentFrame(rollId: Int) = "current_frame_$rollId"
    20→private fun keyMruFilters(rollId: Int) = "mru_filters_$rollId"
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Thin wrapper around SharedPreferences for all app configuration.
    24→ *
    25→ * All photography data lives in Room. This class holds:
    26→ *   - The active roll ID (which loaded roll drives the widget and Quick Screen)
    27→ *   - Onboarding and session state
    28→ *   - User preferences (extra frames, GPS precision, export format, theme)
    29→ *   - Per-roll ephemeral state (current frame pointer, MRU filter chip order)
    30→ *
    31→ * Not a singleton — instantiate wherever needed with an Application or activity Context.
    32→ */
    33→class AppPreferences(context: Context) {
    34→
    35→    private val prefs = context.applicationContext
    36→        .getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
    37→
    38→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→    // Onboarding
    40→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→    /** True after the user completes or skips onboarding. Gates the coach mark flow. */
    43→    var onboardingComplete: Boolean
    44→        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE, false)
    45→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE, value) }
    46→
    47→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→    // Active roll selection
    49→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→    /**
    52→     * The roll ID currently selected for the widget and Quick Screen.
    53→     * -1 means no roll is selected (no loaded rolls exist).
    54→     *
    55→     * Updated by:
    56→     *   - RollSetup when "Create & Load Roll" is tapped (if no other loaded roll exists)
    57→     *   - Quick Screen switch-roll sheet when user selects a different loaded roll
    58→     *   - Automatically when the active roll is finished or unloaded (falls back to next loaded roll)
    59→     */
    60→    var activeRollId: Int
    61→        get() = prefs.getInt(KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID, -1)
    62→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putInt(KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID, value) }
    63→
    64→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→    // Shooting defaults
    66→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    67→
    68→    /**
    69→     * Extra frames appended beyond the film stock's defaultFrameCount at roll creation.
    70→     * The extra frames account for leader frames and the slight over-capacity of most film.
    71→     * Default 2. Editable in Settings.
    72→     */
    73→    var extraFramesPerRoll: Int
    74→        get() = prefs.getInt(KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL, 2)
    75→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putInt(KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL, value) }
    76→
    77→    var gpsPrecision: GpsPrecision
    78→        get() = GpsPrecision.fromKey(prefs.getString(KEY_GPS_PRECISION, GpsPrecision.HIGH.key)!!)
    79→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_GPS_PRECISION, value.key) }
    80→
    81→    var defaultExportFormat: ExportFormat
    82→        get() = ExportFormat.fromKey(
    83→            prefs.getString(KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT, ExportFormat.CSV.key)!!
    84→        )
    85→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT, value.key) }
    86→
    87→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→    // Appearance
    89→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    90→
    91→    var appTheme: AppTheme
    92→        get() = AppTheme.fromKey(prefs.getString(KEY_APP_THEME, AppTheme.SYSTEM.key)!!)
    93→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_APP_THEME, value.key) }
    94→
    95→    /**
    96→     * When true, color-coded elements (e.g. long-exposure shutter speeds) use a supplementary
    97→     * indicator (underline or suffix) in addition to or instead of color alone.
    98→     */
    99→    var accessibleColorMode: Boolean
   100→        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE, false)
   101→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE, value) }
   102→
   103→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104→    // Tip jar
   105→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   106→
   107→    /**
   108→     * True once the one-time 5-rolls-logged prompt has been shown.
   109→     * The tip jar button in Settings remains visible regardless of this flag.
   110→     */
   111→    var tipJarPromptShown: Boolean
   112→        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN, false)
   113→        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN, value) }
   114→
   115→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→    // Per-roll state
   117→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   118→
   119→    /**
   120→     * The current frame number for a roll (1-based). This is the frame the Quick Screen
   121→     * and widget are pointing at for the next log operation.
   122→     *
   123→     * Initialized to 1 when a roll is first loaded. Updated after each successful frame log
   124→     * (advances to the next unlogged frame) and by the frame pointer stepper.
   125→     */
   126→    fun getCurrentFrameNumber(rollId: Int): Int =
   127→        prefs.getInt(keyCurrentFrame(rollId), 1)
   128→
   129→    fun setCurrentFrameNumber(rollId: Int, frameNumber: Int) =
   130→        prefs.edit { putInt(keyCurrentFrame(rollId), frameNumber) }
   131→
   132→    /**
   133→     * Ordered list of up to 4 most recently toggled filter IDs for a roll.
   134→     * Drives the filter chip row on the Quick Screen and Frame Detail screen.
   135→     * Order: most recently used first (index 0).
   136→     */
   137→    fun getMruFilterIds(rollId: Int): List<Int> {
   138→        val raw = prefs.getString(keyMruFilters(rollId), "") ?: ""
   139→        if (raw.isBlank()) return emptyList()
   140→        return raw.split(",").mapNotNull { it.toIntOrNull() }
   141→    }
   142→
   143→    fun setMruFilterIds(rollId: Int, filterIds: List<Int>) {
   144→        prefs.edit { putString(keyMruFilters(rollId), filterIds.take(4).joinToString(",")) }
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    /**
   148→     * Promotes [filterId] to the front of the MRU list for [rollId], keeping at most 4 entries.
   149→     * Called whenever the user toggles a filter on the Quick Screen or Frame Detail screen.
   150→     */
   151→    fun promoteFilterInMru(rollId: Int, filterId: Int) {
   152→        val current = getMruFilterIds(rollId).toMutableList()
   153→        current.remove(filterId)
   154→        current.add(0, filterId)
   155→        setMruFilterIds(rollId, current)
   156→    }
   157→
   158→    /**
   159→     * Removes all per-roll keys for the given roll. Called when a roll is deleted.
   160→     */
   161→    fun clearRollPreferences(rollId: Int) {
   162→        prefs.edit {
   163→            remove(keyCurrentFrame(rollId))
   164→            remove(keyMruFilters(rollId))
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→}
   168→
   169→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170→// Preference enums
   171→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→
   173→enum class GpsPrecision(val key: String) {
   174→    HIGH("high"),
   175→    BATTERY_SAVER("battery_saver");
   176→
   177→    companion object {
   178→        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: HIGH
   179→    }
   180→}
   181→
   182→enum class ExportFormat(val key: String) {
   183→    CSV("csv"),
   184→    JSON("json"),
   185→    PLAIN_TEXT("plain_text");
   186→
   187→    companion object {
   188→        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: CSV
   189→    }
   190→}
   191→
   192→enum class AppTheme(val key: String) {
   193→    LIGHT("light"),
   194→    DARK("dark"),
   195→    SYSTEM("system");
   196→
   197→    companion object {
   198→        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: SYSTEM
   199→    }
   200→}
   201→

     2→
     3→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.ApertureIncrements
     4→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.ShutterIncrements
     5→
     6→/**
     7→ * Generates valid aperture and shutter speed value lists for stepper controls.
     8→ *
     9→ * All values use the canonical storage format (same strings stored in the database):
    10→ *   Apertures: "f/1.4", "f/8", "f/11" etc.
    11→ *   Shutter speeds: "B", "30s", "1s", "1/125", "1/1000" etc.
    12→ *
    13→ * Lists are ordered slow→fast (shutter) or wide→narrow (aperture) so that stepping
    14→ * "up" always increases exposure.
    15→ */
    16→object ExposureValues {
    17→
    18→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→    // Shutter speed sequences
    20→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21→
    22→    // Full stops: B + 19 stops from 30s to 1/8000
    23→    private val SHUTTER_FULL = listOf(
    24→        "B",
    25→        "30s", "15s", "8s", "4s", "2s", "1s",
    26→        "1/2", "1/4", "1/8", "1/15", "1/30", "1/60",
    27→        "1/125", "1/250", "1/500", "1/1000", "1/2000", "1/4000", "1/8000",
    28→    )
    29→
    30→    // Half stops: same range with intermediate values added
    31→    private val SHUTTER_HALF = listOf(
    32→        "B",
    33→        "30s", "20s", "15s", "10s", "8s", "6s", "4s", "3s", "2s", "1.5s", "1s",
    34→        "1/1.5", "1/2", "1/3", "1/4", "1/6", "1/8", "1/10", "1/15", "1/20",
    35→        "1/30", "1/45", "1/60", "1/90", "1/125", "1/180", "1/250", "1/350",
    36→        "1/500", "1/750", "1/1000", "1/1500", "1/2000", "1/3000", "1/4000",
    37→        "1/6000", "1/8000",
    38→    )
    39→
    40→    // Third stops: standard photographic values at 1/3 stop spacing
    41→    private val SHUTTER_THIRD = listOf(
    42→        "B",
    43→        "30s", "25s", "20s", "15s", "13s", "10s", "8s", "6s", "5s", "4s", "3s",
    44→        "2.5s", "2s", "1.6s", "1.3s", "1s",
    45→        "1/1.3", "1/1.6", "1/2", "1/2.5", "1/3", "1/4", "1/5", "1/6",
    46→        "1/8", "1/10", "1/13", "1/15", "1/20", "1/25", "1/30", "1/40", "1/50",
    47→        "1/60", "1/80", "1/100", "1/125", "1/160", "1/200", "1/250", "1/320", "1/400",
    48→        "1/500", "1/640", "1/800", "1/1000", "1/1250", "1/1600", "1/2000", "1/2500",
    49→        "1/3200", "1/4000", "1/5000", "1/6400", "1/8000",
    50→    )
    51→
    52→    /**
    53→     * Returns the complete shutter speed list for the given increment type,
    54→     * ordered slowest → fastest (B first, 1/8000 last).
    55→     */
    56→    fun shutterSpeeds(increments: ShutterIncrements): List<String> = when (increments) {
    57→        ShutterIncrements.FULL -> SHUTTER_FULL
    58→        ShutterIncrements.HALF -> SHUTTER_HALF
    59→        ShutterIncrements.THIRD -> SHUTTER_THIRD
    60→    }

package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity

import androidx.room.Entity
import androidx.room.ForeignKey
import androidx.room.Index
import androidx.room.PrimaryKey

/**
 * A roll of film — the primary shooting journal entity.
 *
 * [isLoaded] distinguishes rolls in a camera (true) from inventory rolls (false). Multiple
 * rolls can be loaded simultaneously (one per camera body). The widget and quick screen reflect
 * whichever loaded roll the user has selected as active — that selection is stored in
 * SharedPreferences, not here.
 *
 * [status] and [isLoaded] are independent:
 *   - active + unloaded: purchased, in inventory, not yet in a camera
 *   - active + loaded: in a camera, actively shooting
 *   - finished: roll is done, camera unloaded
 *   - archived: finished roll moved to archive
 *
 * [pushPull] drives [ratedISO]: ratedISO = FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull.
 * null pushPull indicates a custom ISO override — the user has set ratedISO manually.
 *
 * [totalExposures] is immutable after creation. Calculated at roll creation:
 *   - Standard body: defaultFrameCount + extraFrames
 *   - Half-frame body: (defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) × 2
 * All [Frame] slots are bulk-created at roll creation to match this count.
 */
@Entity(
    tableName = "rolls",
    foreignKeys = [
        ForeignKey(
            entity = FilmStock::class,
            parentColumns = ["id"],
            childColumns = ["filmStockId"],
        ),
        ForeignKey(
            entity = CameraBody::class,
            parentColumns = ["id"],
            childColumns = ["cameraBodyId"],
        ),
    ],
    indices = [Index("filmStockId"), Index("cameraBodyId")],
)
data class Roll(
    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true) val id: Int = 0,
    val name: String,
    val filmStockId: Int,
    val cameraBodyId: Int,
    /**
     * Push/pull stops. Range -3 to +3, default 0 (box speed).
     * Null when the user has entered a custom ratedISO override.
     */
    val pushPull: Int? = 0,
    /** Effective shooting ISO. Derived from FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull, or a custom override. */
    val ratedISO: Int,
    /** Expiry date of this specific physical roll, stored as epoch milliseconds. */
    val filmExpiryDate: Long? = null,
    /** Immutable after creation. See class-level doc for calculation. */
    val totalExposures: Int,
    val isLoaded: Boolean = false,
    /** When true, lat/lng are captured automatically on each frame log. */
    val gpsEnabled: Boolean = false,
    val status: RollStatus = RollStatus.ACTIVE,
    /** When the roll was created, stored as epoch milliseconds. */
    val loadedAt: Long,
    val finishedAt: Long? = null,
    val lastExportedAt: Long? = null,
    val notes: String? = null,
)

    61→
    62→    /**
    63→     * Returns the human-readable display string for a stored shutter speed value.
    64→     *
    65→     * Convention from the DESIGN decisions doc:
    66→     *   "1/125" → "125"  (drop the "1/" — implied by context)
    67→     *   "2s"    → "2s"   (keep the s-suffix for whole seconds)
    68→     *   "B"     → "B"
    69→     */
    70→    fun shutterDisplayValue(stored: String): String = when {
    71→        stored == "B" -> "B"
    72→        stored.endsWith("s") -> stored
    73→        stored.startsWith("1/") -> stored.removePrefix("1/")
    74→        else -> stored // e.g. "1/1.5" keeps as-is if doesn't match above
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    /**
    78→     * Returns true if this shutter speed value should render in the accent color.
    79→     * Whole-second values and bulb are "long exposure" by convention (red numbers on
    80→     * classic cameras like the Canon AE-1).
    81→     */
    82→    fun isLongExposure(stored: String): Boolean =
    83→        stored == "B" || stored.endsWith("s")
    84→
    85→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→    // Aperture sequences
    87→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→
    89→    // Full stops: f/1 through f/64
    90→    private val APERTURE_FULL = listOf(
    91→        1.0f, 1.4f, 2.0f, 2.8f, 4.0f, 5.6f, 8.0f, 11.0f, 16.0f, 22.0f, 32.0f, 45.0f, 64.0f,
    92→    )
    93→
    94→    // Half stops: intermediate values added between full stops
    95→    private val APERTURE_HALF = listOf(
    96→        1.0f, 1.2f, 1.4f, 1.7f, 2.0f, 2.4f, 2.8f, 3.3f, 4.0f, 4.8f, 5.6f, 6.7f,
    97→        8.0f, 9.5f, 11.0f, 13.0f, 16.0f, 19.0f, 22.0f, 27.0f, 32.0f,
    98→    )
    99→
   100→    // Third stops: standard photographic values at 1/3 stop spacing
   101→    private val APERTURE_THIRD = listOf(
   102→        1.0f, 1.1f, 1.2f, 1.4f, 1.6f, 1.8f, 2.0f, 2.2f, 2.5f, 2.8f, 3.2f, 3.5f,
   103→        4.0f, 4.5f, 5.0f, 5.6f, 6.3f, 7.1f, 8.0f, 9.0f, 10.0f, 11.0f, 13.0f, 14.0f,
   104→        16.0f, 18.0f, 20.0f, 22.0f, 25.0f, 29.0f, 32.0f,
   105→    )
   106→
   107→    /**
   108→     * Returns valid aperture strings for a lens, bounded by [maxAperture] (widest) and
   109→     * [minAperture] (narrowest), using the lens's [increments] type.
   110→     *
   111→     * Ordered widest → narrowest (f/1.4 first, f/22 last) — "up" step = wider = more exposure.
   112→     *
   113→     * A small epsilon (0.05) is used in the bounds check to handle floating-point edge cases
   114→     * where lens.maxAperture = 1.4f might not exactly match the list value 1.4f.
   115→     */
   116→    fun apertures(
   117→        maxAperture: Float,
   118→        minAperture: Float,
   119→        increments: ApertureIncrements,
   120→    ): List<String> {
   121→        val all = when (increments) {
   122→            ApertureIncrements.FULL -> APERTURE_FULL
   123→            ApertureIncrements.HALF -> APERTURE_HALF
   124→            ApertureIncrements.THIRD -> APERTURE_THIRD
   125→        }
   126→        return all
   127→            .filter { it >= maxAperture - 0.05f && it <= minAperture + 0.05f }
   128→            .map { formatAperture(it) }
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    /**
   132→     * Formats a Float aperture value to the canonical "f/X" storage string.
   133→     * Integer values omit the decimal point: 8.0f → "f/8", 1.4f → "f/1.4".
   134→     */
   135→    fun formatAperture(value: Float): String =
   136→        if (value == value.toLong().toFloat()) "f/${value.toLong()}" else "f/$value"
   137→
   138→    /**
   139→     * Parses a canonical "f/X" string back to a Float for bounds comparison.
   140→     * Returns null if the string is malformed.
   141→     */
   142→    fun parseAperture(stored: String): Float? =
   143→        stored.removePrefix("f/").toFloatOrNull()
   144→
   145→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   146→    // Exposure compensation
   147→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   148→
   149→    /**
   150→     * Valid exposure compensation values in 1/3 stop increments from -3.0 to +3.0 EV.
   151→     * Null is not in this list — the ViewModel handles null separately as "no EC applied".
   152→     */
   153→    val exposureCompensationValues: List<Float> = buildList {
   154→        // Generate -3.0, -2.67, -2.33 … 0 … 2.33, 2.67, 3.0
   155→        // Use integer arithmetic to avoid float accumulation error.
   156→        for (thirds in -9..9) {
   157→            add(thirds / 3.0f)
   158→        }
   159→    }
   160→
   161→    /**
   162→     * Formats an EC float for display, e.g. 0.0 → "0", 0.333 → "+⅓", -1.0 → "-1".
   163→     * Uses vulgar fractions for third-stop values for a clean camera-dial aesthetic.
   164→     */
   165→    fun formatExposureCompensation(value: Float): String {
   166→        val thirds = (value * 3).toInt()
   167→        val wholePart = thirds / 3
   168→        val remainder = thirds % 3
   169→        val sign = if (value > 0f) "+" else if (value < 0f) "-" else ""
   170→        val absWhole = Math.abs(wholePart)
   171→        val absFrac = Math.abs(remainder)
   172→        return when {
   173→            thirds == 0 -> "0"
   174→            absFrac == 0 -> "$sign$absWhole"
   175→            absWhole == 0 -> "$sign${fraction(absFrac)}"
   176→            else -> "$sign$absWhole${fraction(absFrac)}"
   177→        }
   178→    }
   179→
   180→    private fun fraction(thirds: Int): String = when (thirds) {
   181→        1 -> "⅓"
   182→        2 -> "⅔"
   183→        else -> ""
   184→    }
   185→}

---
name: FrameLog implementation status
description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
type: project
---

## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)

**Database / data layer:**
- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`

**Infrastructure:**
- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport

**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel

---

## UI layer — Gear Library screens complete (verified compiling as of 2026-03-20)

**Done and compiling clean:**
- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; all Gear Library routes wired, Roll/Quick/Settings routes commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController
- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation (Lenses, Bodies, Filters, Film, Kits); ScrollableTabRow, search + sort dropdown, LazyColumn, gear cards for all types, EmptyState; defines ColorType.label (internal)
- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown**, plus ApertureIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines CameraBodyFormat.label and ShutterIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt` — full form; private FilterTypeField autocomplete composable
- `ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` — full form with discontinued Switch; defines FilmFormat.label; reuses ColorType.label from GearLibraryScreen.kt
- `ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt` — full form: body picker, lens add/remove/primary-select, filter toggles, IncompatibleLensesRemoved snackbar, Duplicate text button

**Roll screens — in progress:**
- `ui/rolls/RollListScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - 3-tab layout (Active/Finished/Archived) with counts
  - Active tab splits into "In camera" (loaded) and "Inventory" (unloaded) sections
  - SwipeToDismissBox on unloaded cards → load confirmation dialog
  - Long press context menu (state-aware options: Load Roll, Open Journal, Archive, Unarchive, Delete)
  - 4 AlertDialog confirmations: Load, Delete, Archive, Unarchive
  - `RollListRow` data class (JOIN: Roll + filmStock name + cameraBody name + loggedFrameCount)
  - `dashedBorder` modifier extension for unloaded card treatment
  - `NavGraph.kt` updated: RollList route wired

**Not yet started (needs user go-ahead first):**
- RollSetupScreen, KitSelectorScreen, RollJournalScreen, FrameDetailScreen
- QuickScreen, SettingsScreen, Onboarding screens

---

## Architecture notes

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

input.content:
## Completed this session

RollListScreen complete and compiling. Swipe-to-load on unloaded cards (SwipeToDismissBox, always snaps back). Long press context menu (state-aware: Load Roll, Open Journal, Archive, Unarchive, Delete). Dashed border modifier for unloaded cards. 4 confirmation dialogs. RollListRow data class added (JOIN query: Roll + filmStock name + cameraBody name + loggedFrameCount).

RollJournalScreen complete and compiling. Frame cards: logged (solid border, filled circle, aperture/shutter/lens/EC/GPS/notes) and unlogged (dashed border, empty circle). Current frame gets tertiary accent border + CURRENT label. Long-exposure shutter speeds render in colorScheme.error (red convention). Bottom bar switches by roll state: unloaded active = Load Roll + Export; loaded active = Finish Roll + Export; finished/archived = Export only. Export bottom sheet (ModalBottomSheet): CSV / JSON / Plain Text radio list. 5 confirmation dialogs: Load, Finish Roll (error color, irreversible warning), Archive, Unarchive, Delete (error color). Share export via Intent.ACTION_SEND with content as EXTRA_TEXT.

NavGraph updated: RollList and RollJournal routes both wired.

## Key decision

Per-frame filter data omitted from journal frame cards. RollWithDetails.frames is List<Frame> not List<FrameWithDetails>, so filter names unavailable without an extra query. Full filter data will be shown in FrameDetailScreen.

## Icon set note

Project uses material-icons-core only (no extended). ExpandMore/ExpandLess and LocationOn are NOT in core. Safe substitutions: KeyboardArrowDown/KeyboardArrowUp for sort chevrons, "GPS" text for GPS indicator.

## Next session

RollSetupScreen, FrameDetailScreen, then QuickScreen.

- `ui/rolls/RollListScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - 3-tab layout (Active/Finished/Archived) with counts

  - Header: ISO · push/pull · loaded status subtitle + LinearProgressIndicator (logged/total)

  - Long-exposure shutter speeds render in `colorScheme.error` (red convention)
  - Bottom bar: unloaded active → Load Roll + Export; loaded active → Finish Roll + Export; finished/archived → Export only
  - ExportFormatBottomSheet (ModalBottomSheet): CSV/JSON/Plain Text RadioButton list + Export button

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Build Commands
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→./gradlew assembleDebug          # Build debug APK
     9→./gradlew assembleRelease        # Build release APK
    10→./gradlew test                   # Run unit tests
    11→./gradlew testDebugUnitTest      # Run a single test class: add --tests "com.southsouthwest.framelog.ExampleUnitTest"
    12→./gradlew connectedAndroidTest   # Run instrumented tests (requires device/emulator)
    13→./gradlew lint                   # Run Android Lint
    14→./gradlew clean                  # Clean build artifacts
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Architecture
    18→
    19→Single-module Android app using **Jetpack Compose** with **Material 3**.
    20→
    21→- **Root package**: `com.southsouthwest.framelog`
    22→- **Entry point**: `MainActivity` — sets up Compose content with `FrameLogTheme`
    23→- **Theme**: `ui/theme/` — `Theme.kt` (light/dark + dynamic color on API 31+), `Color.kt`, `Type.kt`
    24→
    25→Key config:
    26→- Min SDK 26, Target/Compile SDK 36
    27→- Kotlin 2.2.10 with Compose compiler plugin
    28→- Dependencies managed via version catalog at `gradle/libs.versions.toml`
    29→- Java 11 compatibility
    30→
    31→# FRAMELOG claude code handoff
    32→## FRAME//LOG — Claude Code Handoff Document
    33→
    34→**Version:** 1.0 **Date:** 2026-03-19 **Prepared by:** Claude (Sonnet 4.6) in conversation with Sean
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## What This Document Is
    39→
    40→This is a complete project handoff for Claude Code. It summarizes all design, architecture, and implementation decisions made during an extended design session covering requirements, data model, API/data layer contracts, screen inventory, and wireframes. Read this document in full before writing any code. The companion Trilium project export contains the full detail documents referenced here.
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→## What FRAME//LOG Is
    45→
    46→A native Android application for film photographers to log shooting data in the field. It is a **shot log**, not a development log, not a light meter, not a social app. Its single core value proposition is that logging a frame should be achievable **one-handed, without unlocking the phone, in under three seconds**.
    47→
    48→The app is designed primarily for the developer's personal use, with potential future public release. It is offline-first, no server, no accounts, no cloud sync in v1.0.
    49→
    50→**Working title:** FRAME//LOG (may change before release)
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Technology Stack
    55→
    56→| Concern | Choice | Notes |
    57→| --- | --- | --- |
    58→| Language | Kotlin | Standard Android |
    59→| UI | Jetpack Compose | Modern Android UI toolkit |
    60→| Database | SQLite via Room | Google's official ORM for Android |
    61→| Navigation | Jetpack Navigation Component | Pass IDs between screens, not objects |
    62→| Widget | Android App Widget API | 2×4 home screen widget |
    63→| GPS | Android Location API | Capture on frame log only, not continuous |
    64→| Export | Android Share Intent | No direct cloud integration |
    65→| Backup | SQLite file export as .framelog | Via share sheet |
    66→| Preferences | SharedPreferences | App config only — not SQLite |
    67→| Haptics | VibrationEffect API | Directional stepper feedback |
    68→| Speech | Android SpeechRecognizer | Note field on Quick Screen |
    69→| Monetization | Ko-fi link or Play in-app purchase | Tip jar only, TBD |
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Project Structure — Recommended
    74→
    75→```
    76→framelog-android/
    77→├── app/
    78→│   ├── src/main/
    79→│   │   ├── java/com/framelog/
    80→│   │   │   ├── data/
    81→│   │   │   │   ├── db/           — Room database, entities, DAOs
    82→│   │   │   │   ├── repository/   — Repository classes
    83→│   │   │   │   └── model/        — Data classes
    84→│   │   │   ├── ui/
    85→│   │   │   │   ├── widget/       — Home screen widget
    86→│   │   │   │   ├── quickscreen/  — Layer 2 quick screen
    87→│   │   │   │   ├── rolls/        — Roll list, setup, journal
    88→│   │   │   │   ├── frames/       — Frame detail/edit
    89→│   │   │   │   ├── gear/         — Gear library screens
    90→│   │   │   │   ├── settings/     — Settings screen
    91→│   │   │   │   └── onboarding/   — Welcome + coach marks
    92→│   │   │   └── MainActivity.kt
    93→│   │   └── res/
    94→│   └── build.gradle
    95→└── README.md
    96→```
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→## Data Model — Complete Entity List
   101→
   102→See individual entity notes in the Trilium export for full field specifications. Summary:
   103→
   104→| Entity | Key Fields | Notes |
   105→| --- | --- | --- |
   106→| CameraBody | id, name, make, model, mountType, format, shutterIncrements, notes | format: `35mm` or `half_frame` |
   107→| Lens | id, name, make, focalLengthMm, mountType, maxAperture, minAperture, apertureIncrements, filterSizeMm, notes | filterSizeMm nullable |
   108→| Filter | id, name, make, filterType, evReduction, filterSizeMm, notes | both nullable fields |
   109→| FilmStock | id, name, make, iso, format, defaultFrameCount, colorType, discontinued, notes | iso = box speed only |
   110→| Kit | id, name, cameraBodyId, lastUsedAt, notes | template only |
   111→| KitLens | kitId, lensId, isPrimary | join table |
   112→| KitFilter | kitId, filterId | join table |
   113→| Roll | id, name, filmStockId, cameraBodyId, pushPull, ratedISO, filmExpiryDate, totalExposures, isLoaded, gpsEnabled, status, loadedAt, finishedAt, lastExportedAt, notes | see Roll notes |
   114→| RollLens | rollId, lensId, isPrimary | join table |
   115→| RollFilter | rollId, filterId | join table |
   116→| Frame | id, rollId, frameNumber, isLogged, loggedAt, aperture, shutterSpeed, lensId, exposureCompensation, lat, lng, notes | frameNumber immutable |
   117→| FrameFilter | frameId, filterId | join table |
   118→
   119→### Critical Roll Fields
   120→
   121→*   `pushPull` — integer -3 to +3, default 0. Drives ratedISO calculation.
   122→*   `ratedISO` — derived from `FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull`. Custom override sets pushPull to null.
   123→*   `isLoaded` — boolean. Distinguishes rolls in a camera (true) from inventory rolls (false).
   124→*   `status` — `active | finished | archived`. Independent of isLoaded.
   125→*   `totalExposures` — immutable after creation. Standard: `defaultFrameCount + extraFrames`. Half-frame: `(defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) × 2`.
   126→
   127→### Critical Frame Fields
   128→
   129→*   `frameNumber` — immutable. Never changes after bulk creation at roll setup.
   130→*   `isLogged` — boolean, independent of loggedAt. Allows retroactive correction without fabricating a timestamp.
   131→*   `aperture` — stored as canonical string e.g. `"f/8"`. UI steppers enforce valid values.
   132→*   `shutterSpeed` — stored as canonical string e.g. `"1/125"`. Display format differs from storage format (see below).
   133→
   134→---
   135→
   136→## DAO Layer — Complete Method List
   137→
   138→See DAO notes in Trilium export for full specifications. Key patterns:
   139→
   140→**All gear DAOs (CameraBodyDao, LensDao, FilterDao, FilmStockDao)** follow identical pattern:
   141→
   142→*   `search[Entity](query: String)` — search-as-you-type
   143→*   `get[Entity]ById(id: Int)` — for detail screens
   144→*   `insert/update/delete[Entity]`
   145→*   Entity-specific: `getDistinctMountTypes()`, `getLensesByMountType()`, `getDistinctFilterTypes()`
   146→
   147→**RollDao** — key methods:
   148→
   149→*   `getActiveRolls()` — all loaded rolls (isLoaded = true)
   150→*   `getRollWithDetails(rollId)` — Roll + RollLens + RollFilter + Frames
   151→*   `getRollForExport(rollId)` — fully denormalized for export
   152→*   `insertRoll()`, `finishRoll()`, `archiveRoll()`, `unarchiveRoll()`, `updateIsLoaded()`, `updateLastExported()`
   153→
   154→**FrameDao:**
   155→
   156→*   `getFramesForRoll(rollId)` — all frames with lens and filters
   157→*   `getFrameById(frameId)` — single frame with lens and filters
   158→*   `insertFrames(frames: List<Frame>)` — bulk insert at roll creation
   159→*   `updateFrame(frame)` — wrapped in transaction with FrameFilter delta
   160→
   161→**KitDao:**
   162→
   163→*   `getKitWithDetails(id)` — Kit + lenses + filters
   164→*   Kit save is a wholesale replace transaction (delete all KitLens/KitFilter, reinsert current set)
   165→
   166→### Transaction Rules
   167→
   168→Three operations must be atomic transactions:
   169→
   170→1.  **Log frame:** updateFrame + insert/delete FrameFilter records (delta only)
   171→2.  **Create roll:** insertRoll + insertRollLens(×n) + insertRollFilter(×n) + insertFrames(bulk)
   172→3.  **Save kit:** insert/updateKit + deleteAllKitLenses + insertKitLens(×n) + deleteAllKitFilters + insertKitFilter(×n)
   173→
   174→### Navigation Pattern
   175→
   176→**Always pass IDs between screens, never objects.** Each screen loads its own data via DAO. This respects Android's navigation size limits and keeps screens self-contained.
   177→
   178→---
   179→
   180→## Storage Architecture
   181→
   182→| Data Type | Storage | Reason |
   183→| --- | --- | --- |
   184→| All photography data | Room/SQLite | Structured, relational |
   185→| Onboarding complete flag | SharedPreferences | Simple boolean |
   186→| App settings/preferences | SharedPreferences | Small config values |
   187→| Active roll selection | SharedPreferences | Which loaded roll is "active" for widget |
   188→
   189→The "active roll" for the widget is stored in SharedPreferences as a rollId. When the user switches rolls via the Quick Screen header, this value updates immediately and the widget re-renders.
   190→
   191→---
   192→
   193→## Screen Architecture
   194→
   195→### App Launch Sequence
   196→
   197→```
