# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build Commands

```bash
./gradlew assembleDebug          # Build debug APK
./gradlew assembleRelease        # Build release APK
./gradlew test                   # Run unit tests
./gradlew testDebugUnitTest      # Run a single test class: add --tests "com.southsouthwest.framelog.ExampleUnitTest"
./gradlew connectedAndroidTest   # Run instrumented tests (requires device/emulator)
./gradlew lint                   # Run Android Lint
./gradlew clean                  # Clean build artifacts
```

## Architecture

Single-module Android app using **Jetpack Compose** with **Material 3**.

- **Root package**: `com.southsouthwest.framelog`
- **Entry point**: `MainActivity` — sets up Compose content with `FrameLogTheme`
- **Theme**: `ui/theme/` — `Theme.kt` (light/dark + dynamic color on API 31+), `Color.kt`, `Type.kt`

Key config:
- Min SDK 26, Target/Compile SDK 36
- Kotlin 2.2.10 with Compose compiler plugin
- Dependencies managed via version catalog at `gradle/libs.versions.toml`
- Java 11 compatibility

# FRAMELOG claude code handoff
## FRAME//LOG — Claude Code Handoff Document

**Version:** 1.0 **Date:** 2026-03-19 **Prepared by:** Claude (Sonnet 4.6) in conversation with Sean

---

## What This Document Is

This is a complete project handoff for Claude Code. It summarizes all design, architecture, and implementation decisions made during an extended design session covering requirements, data model, API/data layer contracts, screen inventory, and wireframes. Read this document in full before writing any code. The companion Trilium project export contains the full detail documents referenced here.

---

## What FRAME//LOG Is

A native Android application for film photographers to log shooting data in the field. It is a **shot log**, not a development log, not a light meter, not a social app. Its single core value proposition is that logging a frame should be achievable **one-handed, without unlocking the phone, in under three seconds**.

The app is designed primarily for the developer's personal use, with potential future public release. It is offline-first, no server, no accounts, no cloud sync in v1.0.

**Working title:** FRAME//LOG (may change before release)

---

## Technology Stack

| Concern | Choice | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Language | Kotlin | Standard Android |
| UI | Jetpack Compose | Modern Android UI toolkit |
| Database | SQLite via Room | Google's official ORM for Android |
| Navigation | Jetpack Navigation Component | Pass IDs between screens, not objects |
| Widget | Android App Widget API | 2×4 home screen widget |
| GPS | Android Location API | Capture on frame log only, not continuous |
| Export | Android Share Intent | No direct cloud integration |
| Backup | SQLite file export as .framelog | Via share sheet |
| Preferences | SharedPreferences | App config only — not SQLite |
| Haptics | VibrationEffect API | Directional stepper feedback |
| Speech | Android SpeechRecognizer | Note field on Quick Screen |
| Monetization | Ko-fi link or Play in-app purchase | Tip jar only, TBD |

---

## Project Structure — Recommended

```
framelog-android/
├── app/
│   ├── src/main/
│   │   ├── java/com/framelog/
│   │   │   ├── data/
│   │   │   │   ├── db/           — Room database, entities, DAOs
│   │   │   │   ├── repository/   — Repository classes
│   │   │   │   └── model/        — Data classes
│   │   │   ├── ui/
│   │   │   │   ├── widget/       — Home screen widget
│   │   │   │   ├── quickscreen/  — Layer 2 quick screen
│   │   │   │   ├── rolls/        — Roll list, setup, journal
│   │   │   │   ├── frames/       — Frame detail/edit
│   │   │   │   ├── gear/         — Gear library screens
│   │   │   │   ├── settings/     — Settings screen
│   │   │   │   └── onboarding/   — Welcome + coach marks
│   │   │   └── MainActivity.kt
│   │   └── res/
│   └── build.gradle
└── README.md
```

---

## Data Model — Complete Entity List

See individual entity notes in the Trilium export for full field specifications. Summary:

| Entity | Key Fields | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CameraBody | id, name, make, model, mountType, format, shutterIncrements, notes | format: `35mm` or `half_frame` |
| Lens | id, name, make, focalLengthMm, mountType, maxAperture, minAperture, apertureIncrements, filterSizeMm, notes | filterSizeMm nullable |
| Filter | id, name, make, filterType, evReduction, filterSizeMm, notes | both nullable fields |
| FilmStock | id, name, make, iso, format, defaultFrameCount, colorType, discontinued, notes | iso = box speed only |
| Kit | id, name, cameraBodyId, lastUsedAt, notes | template only |
| KitLens | kitId, lensId, isPrimary | join table |
| KitFilter | kitId, filterId | join table |
| Roll | id, name, filmStockId, cameraBodyId, pushPull, ratedISO, filmExpiryDate, totalExposures, isLoaded, gpsEnabled, status, loadedAt, finishedAt, lastExportedAt, notes | see Roll notes |
| RollLens | rollId, lensId, isPrimary | join table |
| RollFilter | rollId, filterId | join table |
| Frame | id, rollId, frameNumber, isLogged, loggedAt, aperture, shutterSpeed, lensId, exposureCompensation, lat, lng, notes | frameNumber immutable |
| FrameFilter | frameId, filterId | join table |

### Critical Roll Fields

*   `pushPull` — integer -3 to +3, default 0. Drives ratedISO calculation.
*   `ratedISO` — derived from `FilmStock.iso × 2^pushPull`. Custom override sets pushPull to null.
*   `isLoaded` — boolean. Distinguishes rolls in a camera (true) from inventory rolls (false).
*   `status` — `active | finished | archived`. Independent of isLoaded.
*   `totalExposures` — immutable after creation. Standard: `defaultFrameCount + extraFrames`. Half-frame: `(defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) × 2`.

### Critical Frame Fields

*   `frameNumber` — immutable. Never changes after bulk creation at roll setup.
*   `isLogged` — boolean, independent of loggedAt. Allows retroactive correction without fabricating a timestamp.
*   `aperture` — stored as canonical string e.g. `"f/8"`. UI steppers enforce valid values.
*   `shutterSpeed` — stored as canonical string e.g. `"1/125"`. Display format differs from storage format (see below).

---

## DAO Layer — Complete Method List

See DAO notes in Trilium export for full specifications. Key patterns:

**All gear DAOs (CameraBodyDao, LensDao, FilterDao, FilmStockDao)** follow identical pattern:

*   `search[Entity](query: String)` — search-as-you-type
*   `get[Entity]ById(id: Int)` — for detail screens
*   `insert/update/delete[Entity]`
*   Entity-specific: `getDistinctMountTypes()`, `getLensesByMountType()`, `getDistinctFilterTypes()`

**RollDao** — key methods:

*   `getActiveRolls()` — all loaded rolls (isLoaded = true)
*   `getRollWithDetails(rollId)` — Roll + RollLens + RollFilter + Frames
*   `getRollForExport(rollId)` — fully denormalized for export
*   `insertRoll()`, `finishRoll()`, `archiveRoll()`, `unarchiveRoll()`, `updateIsLoaded()`, `updateLastExported()`

**FrameDao:**

*   `getFramesForRoll(rollId)` — all frames with lens and filters
*   `getFrameById(frameId)` — single frame with lens and filters
*   `insertFrames(frames: List<Frame>)` — bulk insert at roll creation
*   `updateFrame(frame)` — wrapped in transaction with FrameFilter delta

**KitDao:**

*   `getKitWithDetails(id)` — Kit + lenses + filters
*   Kit save is a wholesale replace transaction (delete all KitLens/KitFilter, reinsert current set)

### Transaction Rules

Three operations must be atomic transactions:

1.  **Log frame:** updateFrame + insert/delete FrameFilter records (delta only)
2.  **Create roll:** insertRoll + insertRollLens(×n) + insertRollFilter(×n) + insertFrames(bulk)
3.  **Save kit:** insert/updateKit + deleteAllKitLenses + insertKitLens(×n) + deleteAllKitFilters + insertKitFilter(×n)

### Navigation Pattern

**Always pass IDs between screens, never objects.** Each screen loads its own data via DAO. This respects Android's navigation size limits and keeps screens self-contained.

---

## Storage Architecture

| Data Type | Storage | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| All photography data | Room/SQLite | Structured, relational |
| Onboarding complete flag | SharedPreferences | Simple boolean |
| App settings/preferences | SharedPreferences | Small config values |
| Active roll selection | SharedPreferences | Which loaded roll is "active" for widget |

The "active roll" for the widget is stored in SharedPreferences as a rollId. When the user switches rolls via the Quick Screen header, this value updates immediately and the widget re-renders.

---

## Screen Architecture

### App Launch Sequence

```
App opens
→ Read SharedPreferences: onboardingComplete?
  → No: Welcome Screen
  → Yes: Query Room: any loaded rolls?
    → No: Quick Screen (empty state)
    → Yes: Quick Screen (load active rollId from SharedPreferences)
```

### Key Screen Behaviors

**Quick Screen header** — tappable when multiple loaded rolls exist. Opens Switch Roll bottom sheet. Selecting a roll writes new rollId to SharedPreferences, widget updates.

**Widget** — reflects SharedPreferences activeRollId. On Log Frame tap: writes Frame record, updates FrameFilter delta, auto-advances frame pointer in SharedPreferences.

**Roll Setup** — two action buttons. "Create Roll" sets isLoaded=false. "Create & Load Roll" sets isLoaded=true and writes rollId to SharedPreferences active roll.

**Frame Detail/Edit** — navigated to with frameId + rollId. No prev/next navigation in v1.0. Back arrow triggers save prompt if unsaved changes.

---

## Display Conventions

### Shutter Speed Display

| Stored | Displayed |
| --- | --- |
| `"1/1000"` | `1000` |
| `"1/125"` | `125` |
| `"1/30"` | `30` |
| `"1s"` | `1s` |
| `"2s"` | `2s` |
| `"B"` | `B` |

Whole-second values and B displayed in accent color (red convention from film cameras). Color blind mode substitutes underline or suffix indicator.

### Aperture Display

Stored as `"f/8"`, displayed as `"f/8"`. Input in gear detail: `f/` fixed prefix + plain number entry.

### Filter EV Sum

Displayed on Quick Screen and Frame Detail filter row. Sum of non-null evReduction values for active filters. `~` prefix if any active filter has null evReduction. Hidden when no filters active.

### Push/Pull Display

| pushPull value | Display |
| --- | --- |
| 0 | "box speed" |
| 1 | "push 1 · ISO 800" |
| \-1 | "pull 1 · ISO 200" |
| null (custom) | ISO value only |

---

## Haptics

All stepper controls use directional haptic feedback:

*   `+` (increment) — single firm haptic (`VibrationEffect.createOneShot`)
*   `−` (decrement) — double short pulse (`VibrationEffect.createWaveform`)
*   Log Frame confirmation — distinct longer haptic on successful write

Respect system accessibility settings — check if haptics are enabled before triggering.

---

## Roll Loading Interaction Matrix

| Surface | Interaction | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Roll List card (unloaded) | Tap | Open Roll Journal |
| Roll List card (unloaded) | Swipe left or right | Load Roll confirmation sheet |
| Roll List card (unloaded) | Long press | Context menu: Load Roll, Open Journal, Delete |
| Roll List card (loaded) | Long press | Context menu: Open Journal, Delete |
| Roll List card (finished) | Long press | Context menu: Open Journal, Archive, Delete |
| Roll List card (archived) | Long press | Context menu: Open Journal, Unarchive, Delete |
| Roll Journal (unloaded) | Load Roll button | Load Roll confirmation sheet |
| Roll Journal (any) | Overflow ⋯ | Archive / Unarchive / Delete |

All destructive and state-change actions require confirmation sheets. Delete is always a danger confirmation (permanent, cannot be undone).

---

## Onboarding

Coach mark pattern — overlay on real UI, user performs real interactions. Five steps:

1.  Gear Library → Lenses → tap +
2.  Gear Library → Bodies → tap +
3.  Gear Library → Film Stocks → tap +
4.  Roll List → tap FAB → Roll Setup → Create & Load Roll
5.  Settings → Widget Setup Instructions

SharedPreferences boolean `onboardingComplete` gates the flow. Re-runnable from Settings.

Tip jar prompt triggers once at 5 rolls logged. Never again after that. Button always available in Settings.

---

## v1.0 Scope Boundaries — Hard Limits

Do not implement these in v1.0 regardless of how easy they seem:

*   Medium format (reserve `medium_format` enum value but do not surface in UI)
*   Zoom lenses (focalLengthMm is single value only)
*   Stepless aperture / cine lenses
*   Google Sheets sync
*   ExifTool command file export
*   Per-frame ISO override
*   In-app map rendering (use geo intent to open user's maps app)
*   Background GPS tracking
*   iOS support

---

## Legal Requirements (before Play Store submission)

*   **Privacy policy** required — generates at app-privacy-policy-generator.nisrulz.com. Host at GitHub Pages. Link from Settings and Play Store listing.
*   **Open source licenses** — use Google Play Services OSS Licenses Gradle plugin. Auto-generates from dependencies.

---

## Reference Documents in Trilium Export

All detail is in these companion documents. Read them as needed during implementation:

| Document | Contents |
| --- | --- |
| FRAMELOG PRD v0.4 | Full requirements, scope, layer specs |
| 00\_data\_model\_overview | Entity list, relationships, key constraints |
| 01\_camerabody through 10\_kitlens\_kitfilter | Individual entity schemas with all fields |
| DAO\_00\_overview through DAO\_04\_kitdao | Complete DAO method specifications |
| FRAMELOG\_screen\_inventory | Every screen, every element, all behaviors |
| FRAMELOG\_navigation\_map.mermaid | Full navigation diagram |
| DESIGN\_decisions | All functional design decisions from wireframing |
| WIREFRAME\_\*.svg | Visual reference for all screens |

---

## A Note on Code Quality

This app is intended for potential public release on the Play Store. Code should be written as if it will be read and audited by the developer, who is learning Android development and will review all generated code before shipping. Prefer clarity over cleverness. Comment non-obvious decisions. Follow Android architecture guidelines (MVVM, repository pattern, unidirectional data flow).

The developer's primary concern about AI-generated code is security and correctness — particularly around data handling and file I/O. Be explicit about what each piece of code does and why.

1→# FRAMELOG screen inventory
     2→## Screen Inventory
     3→
     4→## Overview
     5→
     6→FRAME//LOG contains approximately 26 distinct screens across 5 sections. Most complexity is concentrated in the one-time gear setup layer. Daily field use touches 2-4 screens maximum.
     7→
     8→Many screens follow the same list/detail pattern repeated for different entity types — the unique screen template count is closer to 8-10.
     9→
    10→See `FRAMELOG_navigation_map.mermaid` for the full navigation diagram.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Onboarding (first run only)
    15→
    16→| Screen | Description | Notes |
    17→| --- | --- | --- |
    18→| Welcome Screen | App name, tagline, "show me around" / "skip setup" buttons | Sets onboarding boolean flag |
    19→| Add First Lens | Gear library lens edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen |
    20→| Add First Camera Body | Gear library body edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen — mount type picker already populated from lens |
    21→| Add First Film Stock | Gear library stock edit screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses gear library screen |
    22→| Load First Roll | Roll setup screen with coach mark overlay | Reuses roll setup screen |
    23→| Widget Setup Instructions | Step-by-step guide to adding 2×4 widget to home screen | Android cannot add widget programmatically. Also accessible from Settings anytime. |
    24→
    25→**Coach mark pattern:** Semi-transparent overlay with highlighted cutout, arrow, step counter, "got it ›" button, and "skip tour" link. User performs real interactions — coach mark does not perform actions on their behalf.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Bottom Navigation — 5 Destinations
    30→
    31→| Position | Destination | Layer |
    32→| --- | --- | --- |
    33→| 1 (left) | Gear Library | 4 |
    34→| 2 | Roll List | 3 |
    35→| 3 (center) | Quick Screen | 2 |
    36→| 4 | Active Roll Journal | 3 |
    37→| 5 (right) | Settings | — |
    38→
    39→Quick Screen is center position — primary daily destination, thumb-reachable without looking.
    40→
    41→---
    42→
    43→## Layer 2 — Quick Screen
    44→
    45→Single screen. App home. All interactive elements in bottom 60% for one-handed use.
    46→
    47→**Header (tappable when multiple loaded rolls exist)**
    48→
    49→| Element | Type | Notes |
    50→| --- | --- | --- |
    51→| "active roll · tap to switch" | Muted label | Only shown when multiple loaded rolls exist |
    52→| Roll name | Tappable, underlined with › | Opens Switch Roll bottom sheet |
    53→| Filter count + EV comp | Read-only, right-aligned | Status indicators |
    54→| Frame counter | Read-only | Current / total |
    55→
    56→**Switch Roll Bottom Sheet** — appears on header tap. Shows all loaded rolls with radio buttons, frame progress bars, and "last shot" timestamp. No confirmation needed — selection is immediately reversible.
    57→
    58→**Interactive fields (top to bottom — frequency order)**
    59→
    60→| Element | Type | Notes |
    61→| --- | --- | --- |
    62→| Lens selector | Tap to cycle | Cycles through roll's configured lenses |
    63→| Filter chips (up to 4 MRU) + EV sum + picker | Tap to toggle / + to open picker | MRU = most recently used. EV sum right-aligned, ~ prefix if any filter has null evReduction |
    64→| Exposure compensation | Centered stepper | ±3 EV in 1/3 stop increments, nullable |
    65→| Frame pointer | Centered stepper | Manual navigation between frame slots |
    66→| Aperture | Large centered stepper | Bounded by lens min/max, increments from lens |
    67→| Shutter speed | Large centered stepper | Increments from camera body. Whole seconds in distinct color. |
    68→| Note | Text field + mic button | Optional, speech to text available |
    69→| Log Frame | Primary action button | Full width, anchored to bottom |
    70→
    71→All fields pre-populate from last logged frame. Overwriting a previously logged frame triggers confirmation prompt.
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Layer 3 — Roll Management
    76→
    77→### Roll List Screen
    78→
    79→Entry point for all rolls. FAB (+) bottom right to create a new roll.
    80→
    81→**Tabs:** Active / Finished / Archived (swipe to navigate). Tab shows count when inside (e.g. "Active (3)").
    82→
    83→**Active tab sections:**
    84→
    85→*   Loaded rolls (camera icon, solid border)
    86→*   Unloaded rolls (canister icon, dashed border, muted, "← swipe to load →" hint)
    87→
    88→**Card interactions:**
    89→
    90→*   Tap → Roll Journal View
    91→*   Swipe left or right → Load Roll confirmation sheet (unloaded rolls only)
    92→*   Long press → context-aware quick action menu (options vary by roll state)
    93→
    94→**Long press menu by state:**
    95→
    96→| Roll State | Available Actions |
    97→| --- | --- |
    98→| Active, unloaded | Load Roll (→ confirmation), Open Journal, Delete (→ danger) |
    99→| Active, loaded | Open Journal, Delete (→ danger) |
   100→| Finished | Open Journal, Archive (→ confirmation), Delete (→ danger) |
   101→| Archived | Open Journal, Unarchive (→ confirmation), Delete (→ danger) |
   102→
   103→### Roll Setup Screen
   104→
   105→Single scrollable form. Two-handed, unhurried. Accessed via FAB on Roll List and during onboarding.
   106→
   107→| Section | Fields |
   108→| --- | --- |
   109→| Film stock | Select from library or create new |
   110→|  | Frame count (defaults from stock, overridable) |
   111→|  | Push/pull actuator (Pull 3 → Box → Push 3, ISO updates live) |
   112→|  | Custom ISO override (collapsed link below actuator) |
   113→|  | Film expiry date (optional) |
   114→| Kit (optional) | Load from kit button — pre-fills body, lenses, filters. All editable after. |
   115→| Camera body | Select from library |
   116→| Lenses | Primary lens (required), additional lenses optional. Filtered by mount type. |
   117→| Filters | Chip pattern, optional |
   118→| Details | GPS capture toggle |
   119→|  | Roll name (auto-generated, editable) |
   120→| Actions | Create Roll (unloaded) / Create & Load Roll (loaded) |
   121→
   122→### Kit Selector Screen
   123→
   124→Accessed from Roll Setup. Searchable list of kits. Read-only selection surface.
   125→
   126→*   Tap kit → pre-populates roll setup, returns to form
   127→*   FAB → create new kit (navigates to Kit Detail, returns on save)
   128→*   "Skip — fill gear manually" button at bottom
   129→
   130→### Roll Journal View
   131→
   132→Scrollable list of all frame slots for a specific roll.
   133→
   134→**Header:** Roll name, back arrow (←), overflow menu (⋯), progress bar, film stock + body + ISO info.
   135→
   136→**Frame cards — hybrid pattern:**
   137→
   138→*   Logged: filled circle + frame number + solid border + aperture, shutter, lens, filters, timestamp, GPS indicator, note preview
   139→*   Unlogged: empty circle + dashed border + "— unlogged —"
   140→*   Current frame: heavier border + "current" label
   141→*   Extra frames labeled "(extra)"
   142→
   143→**Bottom bar — state dependent:**
   144→
   145→| Roll State | Bottom Bar |
   146→| --- | --- |
   147→| Active, unloaded | Load Roll (primary) + Export |
   148→| Active, loaded | Finish Roll (primary) + Export |
   149→| Finished | Export only |
   150→| Archived | Export only |
   151→
   152→**Overflow menu (⋯):** Archive / Unarchive (state dependent), Delete (→ danger confirmation always).
   153→
   154→**Roll status transitions:**
   155→
   156→*   Active (unloaded) → Active (loaded): Load Roll — confirmation required
   157→*   Active (loaded) → Finished: Finish Roll — confirmation required, irreversible
   158→*   Finished → Archived: Archive — confirmation required
   159→*   Archived → Finished: Unarchive — confirmation required
   160→*   Any state → Deleted: Delete — danger confirmation, permanent
   161→
   162→### Frame Detail / Edit Screen
   163→
   164→Same fields as Quick Screen (minus frame pointer), pre-populated. Always editable.
   165→
   166→*   `isLogged` toggle — marks frame as logged without fabricating a timestamp (retroactive correction)
   167→*   GPS coordinates displayed as tappable link — opens user's preferred maps app via Android geo intent
   168→*   Note field slightly taller than Quick Screen — unhurried context
   169→*   Save Changes button (no auto-advance)
   170→*   Back arrow triggers save prompt if unsaved changes exist
   171→*   Overflow menu (⋯): no additional actions in v1.0
   172→
   173→---
   174→
   175→## Layer 4 — Gear Library
   176→
   177→Five tabs, identical list/detail pattern. All list screens: search bar + sort dropdown, scrollable cards, FAB.
   178→
   179→| Tab | Entity | Sort Options |
   180→| --- | --- | --- |
   181→| Lenses | Lens | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added, Mount Type |
   182→| Bodies | CameraBody | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |
   183→| Filters | Filter | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added, Filter Size |
   184→| Film Stocks | FilmStock | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |
   185→| Kits | Kit | A–Z, Last Used, Recently Added |
   186→
   187→**Design principle:** All list screens follow the same pattern — search bar + sort, scrollable results, FAB to add, tap to detail. No exceptions in v1.0.
   188→
   189→**Gear Detail / Edit screens:** Full CRUD. Delete and Duplicate in overflow menu (⋯) only. Input patterns: `f/` prefix for aperture values, `mm` suffix for filter size, autocomplete for folksonomy fields (mount type, filter type).
   190→
   191→**Kit Detail / Edit:** Radio buttons for primary lens designation. Camera body change automatically removes incompatible lenses with warning to review filters.
   192→
   193→---
   194→
   195→## Settings
   196→
   197→| Section | Screen / Setting | Notes |
   198→| --- | --- | --- |
   199→| Shooting Defaults | Extra frames per roll | Integer, default 2 |
   200→|  | GPS precision | High / battery saver |
   201→|  | Default export format | CSV / JSON / plain text |
   202→| Appearance | App theme | Light / dark / system |
   203→|  | Accessible color mode | Off by default — alternative indicators for color-coded elements |
   204→| Data & Backup | Export backup | Writes .framelog file via share sheet |
   205→|  | Restore from backup | Destructive — requires confirmation |
   206→| Widget | Widget setup instructions | Same screen as onboarding step 6 |
   207→| Onboarding | Re-run introduction | Resets onboarding boolean flag |
   208→| Support | Tip jar | Ko-fi link or Play in-app purchase |
   209→| About | Version | Read-only |
   210→|  | Privacy policy | External link |
   211→|  | Open source licenses | Generated by OSS Licenses plugin |
   212→
   213→---
   214→
   215→## Export Flow
   216→
   217→Triggered from Roll Journal View. Bottom sheet — not a full screen.
   218→
   219→| Format | v1.0 |
   220→| --- | --- |
   221→| CSV | Yes |
   222→| JSON | Yes |
   223→| Plain text | Yes |
   224→| Google Sheets | v1.1 |
   225→| ExifTool command file | v1.1 |
   226→
   227→Format selection via radio buttons. Export button hands file to Android share sheet — user chooses destination (Drive, email, Files, etc.). No direct cloud integration in v1.0.
   228→
   229→---
   230→
   231→## Screen Count Summary
   232→
   233→| Section | Screens |
   234→| --- | --- |
   235→| Onboarding | 6 (4 reuse existing screens) |
   236→| Quick Screen | 1 + Switch Roll bottom sheet |
   237→| Roll Management | 5 (Roll List, Roll Setup, Kit Selector, Roll Journal, Frame Detail) |
   238→| Gear Library | 10 (5 list + 5 detail/edit) |
   239→| Settings | 1 (with sub-sections) |
   240→| Export | 1 (bottom sheet) |
   241→| **Total unique screens** | **~24 + 2 bottom sheets** |

   367→    private suspend fun createRoll(loadAfterCreate: Boolean) {

   372→

   388→
   389→        val rollLenses = s.selectedLenses.map { row ->
   390→            RollLens(rollId = 0, lensId = row.lens.id, isPrimary = row.isPrimary)
   391→        }
   392→        val rollFilters = s.selectedFilters.map { filter ->
   393→            RollFilter(rollId = 0, filterId = filter.id)
   394→        }
   395→        val frames = (1..s.totalExposures).map { frameNumber ->
   396→            Frame(id = 0, rollId = 0, frameNumber = frameNumber)
   397→        }
   398→
   399→        val newRollId = rollRepository.createRollWithAssociations(roll, rollLenses, rollFilters, frames)
   400→
   401→        // If loaded and no active roll is set, make this the active roll
   402→        if (loadAfterCreate && appPreferences.activeRollId == -1) {
   403→            appPreferences.activeRollId = newRollId.toInt()
   404→        }
   405→
   406→        // If this kit was used, update its lastUsedAt
   407→        // (kit selection not tracked in this ViewModel — handled by KitDetailViewModel)
   408→
   409→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = false) }
   410→
   411→        val event = if (loadAfterCreate) {
   412→            RollSetupEvent.RollCreatedAndLoaded(newRollId.toInt())
   413→        } else {
   414→            RollSetupEvent.RollCreated(newRollId.toInt())
   415→        }
   416→        _events.send(event)
   417→    }
   418→
   419→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   420→    // Helpers
   421→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   422→
   423→    private fun computeRatedISO(boxISO: Int, pushPull: Int): Int =
   424→        (boxISO * 2.0.pow(pushPull.toDouble())).roundToInt()
   425→
   426→    private fun computeTotalExposures(
   427→        frameCount: Int,
   428→        extraFrames: Int,
   429→        isHalfFrame: Boolean,
   430→    ): Int {
   431→        val base = frameCount + extraFrames
   432→        return if (isHalfFrame) base * 2 else base
   433→    }
   434→
   435→    private fun generateRollName(stock: FilmStock): String {
   436→        val date = SimpleDateFormat("MMM ''yy", Locale.US).format(Date())
   437→        return "${stock.make} ${stock.name} — $date"
   438→    }
   439→
   440→    private fun validate(s: RollSetupUiState): Boolean {
   441→        var valid = true
   442→        if (s.selectedFilmStock == null) {
   443→            _state.update { it.copy(filmStockError = "Select a film stock") }
   444→            valid = false
   445→        }
   446→        if (s.selectedCameraBody == null) {
   447→            _state.update { it.copy(cameraBodyError = "Select a camera body") }
   448→            valid = false
   449→        }
   450→        if (s.selectedLenses.isEmpty()) {
   451→            _state.update { it.copy(lensesError = "Add at least one lens") }
   452→            valid = false
   453→        }
   454→        return valid
   455→    }

    16→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.receiveAsFlow
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.stateIn
    18→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
    19→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
    20→
    21→data class KitSelectorUiState(
    22→    val kits: List<KitWithDetails> = emptyList(),
    23→    val searchQuery: String = "",
    24→    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
    25→)
    26→
    27→sealed class KitSelectorEvent {
    28→    /** User selected a kit — navigate back to Roll Setup with this kit's data pre-filled. */
    29→    data class KitSelected(val kit: KitWithDetails) : KitSelectorEvent()
    30→    /** User tapped FAB to create a new kit; navigate to Kit Detail, return on save. */
    31→    data object NavigateToNewKit : KitSelectorEvent()
    32→}
    33→
    34→class KitSelectorViewModel(application: Application) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    35→
    36→    private val kitRepository = KitRepository(AppDatabase.getInstance(application))
    37→
    38→    private val _searchQuery = MutableStateFlow("")
    39→
    40→    // Kits are loaded as KitWithDetails so the selector card can show the full gear preview
    41→    // (body name, lens names, filter chips) without additional queries.
    42→    // Note: KitDao.searchKits returns Kit, not KitWithDetails. We need KitWithDetails per kit.
    43→    // Since there's no "searchKitsWithDetails" query, we search kits and then resolve details.
    44→    // For simplicity, load all kits with details and filter in the ViewModel.
    45→    private val _allKits = MutableStateFlow<List<KitWithDetails>>(emptyList())
    46→
    47→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(KitSelectorUiState())
    48→    val state: StateFlow<KitSelectorUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    49→
    50→    private val _events = Channel<KitSelectorEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    51→    val events: Flow<KitSelectorEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    52→
    53→    init {
    54→        // Collect the full kit list (search within ViewModel since KitWithDetails has no DAO search)
    55→        viewModelScope.launch {
    56→            kitRepository.searchKits("").collect { kits ->
    57→                // Resolve each Kit to KitWithDetails using .first() on the Flow so we
    58→                // take only one DB snapshot and don't block indefinitely on the collector.
    59→                val detailed = kits.mapNotNull { kit ->
    60→                    try {
    61→                        kitRepository.getKitWithDetails(kit.id).first()
    62→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
    63→                        null
    64→                    }
    65→                }
    66→                _allKits.value = detailed
    67→                applyFilter()
    68→            }
    69→        }
    70→
    71→        viewModelScope.launch {
    72→            _searchQuery.debounce(300).collect {
    73→                applyFilter()
    74→            }
    75→        }
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    private fun applyFilter() {
    79→        val query = _searchQuery.value.trim().lowercase()
    80→        val filtered = if (query.isBlank()) {
    81→            _allKits.value
    82→        } else {
    83→            _allKits.value.filter { kit ->
    84→                kit.kit.name.lowercase().contains(query) ||
    85→                    kit.cameraBody.name.lowercase().contains(query) ||
    86→                    kit.cameraBody.make.lowercase().contains(query)
    87→            }
    88→        }
    89→        _state.update {
    90→            it.copy(kits = filtered, searchQuery = _searchQuery.value, isLoading = false)
    91→        }
    92→    }
    93→
    94→    fun onSearchQueryChanged(query: String) {
    95→        _searchQuery.value = query
    96→        _state.update { it.copy(searchQuery = query) }
    97→    }
    98→
    99→    fun onKitSelected(kit: KitWithDetails) = viewModelScope.launch {
   100→        _events.send(KitSelectorEvent.KitSelected(kit))
   101→    }
   102→
   103→    fun onCreateNewKitTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   104→        _events.send(KitSelectorEvent.NavigateToNewKit)
   105→    }
   106→}
   107→

---
name: FrameLog implementation status
description: Full implementation status of the FRAME//LOG Android app — data, VM, and UI layers
type: project
---

## Fully complete layers (compiling cleanly as of 2026-03-19)

**Database / data layer:**
- All 12 Room entities in `data/db/entity/`
- All enums in `Enums.kt`: CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat, ShutterIncrements, ApertureIncrements, ColorType, RollStatus
- TypeConverters for all enums (string values, not ordinals)
- All 11 DAOs in `data/db/dao/`
- Relation types in `data/db/relation/`: RollWithDetails, FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, + join relations
- Export types in `RollExport.kt`
- AppDatabase: singleton, all DAO accessors, 3 multi-DAO @Transaction methods
- All 4 repositories in `data/repository/`

**Infrastructure:**
- `data/AppPreferences.kt` — SharedPreferences wrapper (activeRollId, onboarding, settings, per-roll currentFrame + MRU filters)
- `ui/util/ExposureValues.kt` — shutter speed + aperture value lists, display formatters, EC values
- `ui/util/ExportFormatter.kt` — CSV, JSON, plain text formatters for RollExport

**ViewModel layer (13 VMs):**
GearLibraryViewModel, LensDetailViewModel, CameraBodyDetailViewModel, FilterDetailViewModel,
FilmStockDetailViewModel, KitDetailViewModel, KitSelectorViewModel, RollListViewModel,
RollSetupViewModel, RollJournalViewModel, FrameDetailViewModel, QuickScreenViewModel, SettingsViewModel

---

## UI layer — Gear Library screens complete (verified compiling as of 2026-03-20)

**Done and compiling clean:**
- `ui/navigation/NavRoutes.kt` — all type-safe @Serializable route definitions (Navigation Compose 2.8.0)
- `ui/navigation/NavGraph.kt` — FrameLogNavGraph NavHost; all Gear Library routes wired, Roll/Quick/Settings routes commented as TODO
- `MainActivity.kt` — updated to use FrameLogNavGraph + rememberNavController
- `ui/gear/GearLibraryScreen.kt` — full 5-tab implementation (Lenses, Bodies, Filters, Film, Kits); ScrollableTabRow, search + sort dropdown, LazyColumn, gear cards for all types, EmptyState; defines ColorType.label (internal)
- `ui/gear/LensDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines shared internal composables **MountTypeField** and **EnumDropdown**, plus ApertureIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/CameraBodyDetailScreen.kt` — full form; defines CameraBodyFormat.label and ShutterIncrements.label
- `ui/gear/FilterDetailScreen.kt` — full form; private FilterTypeField autocomplete composable
- `ui/gear/FilmStockDetailScreen.kt` — full form with discontinued Switch; defines FilmFormat.label; reuses ColorType.label from GearLibraryScreen.kt
- `ui/gear/KitDetailScreen.kt` — full form: body picker, lens add/remove/primary-select, filter toggles, IncompatibleLensesRemoved snackbar, Duplicate text button

**Roll screens — in progress:**
- `ui/rolls/RollListScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - 3-tab layout (Active/Finished/Archived) with counts
  - Active tab splits into "In camera" (loaded) and "Inventory" (unloaded) sections
  - SwipeToDismissBox on unloaded cards → load confirmation dialog
  - Long press context menu (state-aware options: Load Roll, Open Journal, Archive, Unarchive, Delete)
  - 4 AlertDialog confirmations: Load, Delete, Archive, Unarchive
  - `RollListRow` data class (JOIN: Roll + filmStock name + cameraBody name + loggedFrameCount)
  - `dashedBorder` modifier extension for unloaded card treatment
  - `NavGraph.kt` updated: RollList route wired
- `ui/rolls/RollJournalScreen.kt` — complete and compiling (2026-03-20)
  - TopAppBar (back, roll name, overflow ⋯ with Archive/Unarchive/Delete)
  - Header: ISO · push/pull · loaded status subtitle + LinearProgressIndicator (logged/total)
  - Search OutlinedTextField + sort dropdown (frame 1→N / N→1) via KeyboardArrowDown/Up
  - LazyColumn of FrameCards: logged (solid border, filled circle, aperture/shutter/lens/EC/GPS/notes) and unlogged (dashed border, empty circle, "— unlogged —"); current frame gets tertiary accent border + "CURRENT" label
  - Long-exposure shutter speeds render in `colorScheme.error` (red convention)
  - Bottom bar: unloaded active → Load Roll + Export; loaded active → Finish Roll + Export; finished/archived → Export only
  - ExportFormatBottomSheet (ModalBottomSheet): CSV/JSON/Plain Text RadioButton list + Export button
  - 5 AlertDialog confirmations: Load, Finish Roll (error-colored, irreversible warning), Archive, Unarchive, Delete (error-colored)
  - Share export via `Intent.ACTION_SEND` (content as EXTRA_TEXT)
  - `NavGraph.kt` updated: RollJournal route wired

**Icon set note:** Project uses material-icons-core only (no extended). Confirmed safe core icons: ArrowBack (AutoMirrored.Filled), Search, Clear, MoreVert, KeyboardArrowDown, KeyboardArrowUp. ExpandMore/ExpandLess, LocationOn are NOT in core — substituted with KeyboardArrow icons and "GPS" text respectively.

**Not yet started (needs user go-ahead first):**
- RollSetupScreen, KitSelectorScreen, FrameDetailScreen
- QuickScreen, SettingsScreen, Onboarding screens

---

## Architecture notes

- No DI framework — repositories get AppDatabase passed directly
- Navigation: type-safe routes via @Serializable data classes; SavedStateHandle auto-populated with route params by Navigation 2.8.0
- ViewModel creation: `viewModel()` inside `composable<T>` — Navigation Compose handles Application + SavedStateHandle injection automatically
- Start destination is GearLibrary temporarily; will move to QuickScreen with onboarding/launch logic later
- MediumFormat excluded from all v1.0 UI (CameraBodyFormat, FilmFormat)
- `collectAsStateWithLifecycle()` used in screen composables (requires lifecycle-runtime-compose; available via lifecycle 2.8.0 BOM)

**Why:** App is offline-first, no server. data/model/ dir not created — entities serve as models directly.
**How to apply:** Wait for explicit go-ahead from user before starting large implementation blocks. Repository layer is the correct access point for ViewModels.

content.text:
`RollSetupScreen.kt` and `KitSelectorScreen.kt` are done, both compiling clean. Here's what was built:

**RollSetupScreen — form sections:**
- **Film Stock:** picker (ModalBottomSheet), frame count field with "N extra = N slots" display, push/pull stepper (−/box/+N with live ISO label), film expiry date (DatePickerDialog with Clear button)
- **Kit:** "Load from kit" row → navigates to KitSelector
- **Camera Body:** picker (ModalBottomSheet), shows mount type hint next to Lenses header
- **Lenses:** RadioButton-per-row (primary), Clear remove button, "+ add another lens" button (disabled if no body) → filtered lens picker
- **Filters:** FlowRow of FilterChips, all togglable
- **Details:** GPS Switch with description, editable roll name field
- **Custom ISO:** collapsed link (underlined text) that expands to a rated ISO text field, bypassing push/pull
- **Actions:** "Create Roll" (outlined) + "Create + Load" (filled primary)

**KitSelectorScreen:** searchable kit list, shares `RollSetupViewModel` via `navController.getBackStackEntry(RollSetup::class)` — so `onKitSelected(kit)` is called directly without serialization.

**One known TODO:** `RollCreatedAndLoaded` navigates to `RollJournal` for now — will redirect to `QuickScreen` once that's implemented.

Perfect. I found the Frame Detail screen section in the inventory. Here is the complete Frame Detail / Edit Screen specification:

---

## Frame Detail / Edit Screen (Lines 162-171)

**Location:** Layer 3 — Roll Management

**Overview:**
Same fields as Quick Screen (minus frame pointer), pre-populated. Always editable.

**Fields and Components:**

- **isLogged toggle** — marks frame as logged without fabricating a timestamp (retroactive correction)
- **GPS coordinates** — displayed as tappable link that opens user's preferred maps app via Android geo intent
- **Note field** — slightly taller than Quick Screen for unhurried context
- **Lens selector** — inherited from Quick Screen pattern
- **Filter chips** — with EV sum and picker (inherited from Quick Screen)
- **Exposure compensation** — centered stepper (±3 EV in 1/3 stop increments, nullable)
- **Aperture** — large centered stepper bounded by lens min/max, increments from lens
- **Shutter speed** — large centered stepper with increments from camera body; whole seconds displayed in distinct color
- **Save Changes button** — primary action (no auto-advance)

**Navigation and Behaviors:**

- Back arrow triggers save prompt if unsaved changes exist
- Overflow menu (⋯): no additional actions in v1.0
- Accessed from Roll Journal View by tapping frame cards
- Navigated to with frameId + rollId parameters

**Key Design Notes:**

- Frame Detail is a dedicated editing surface (unlike Quick Screen, which is the logging interface)
- Always editable (contrast with Quick Screen's frame pointer for navigation)
- GPS intent opens native maps app rather than embedding a map
- Allows retroactive correction of frame logging status independent of timestamp

1→package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.frames
     2→
     3→import android.app.Application
     4→import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
     5→import androidx.lifecycle.SavedStateHandle
     6→import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
     7→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.AppPreferences
     8→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.AppDatabase
     9→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Filter
    10→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Frame
    11→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.FrameFilter
    12→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Lens
    13→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.relation.FrameWithDetails
    14→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.repository.FrameRepository
    15→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.repository.RollRepository
    16→import com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.util.ExposureValues
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.Channel
    18→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
    19→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
    20→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
    21→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
    22→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.first
    23→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.receiveAsFlow
    24→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
    25→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
    26→
    27→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→// UiState
    29→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→data class FrameDetailUiState(
    32→    val frame: FrameWithDetails? = null,
    33→    // Lenses available on this roll (for the lens picker)
    34→    val rollLenses: List<Lens> = emptyList(),
    35→    // Filters available on this roll (for the filter picker and chips)
    36→    val rollFilters: List<Filter> = emptyList(),
    37→    // Valid aperture values for the selected lens (drives the stepper)
    38→    val availableApertures: List<String> = emptyList(),
    39→    // Valid shutter speeds for the roll's camera body (drives the stepper)
    40→    val availableShutterSpeeds: List<String> = emptyList(),
    41→
    42→    // Editable draft — initialized from frame.frame on first load
    43→    val isLogged: Boolean = false,
    44→    val selectedLensId: Int? = null,
    45→    val activeFilterIds: Set<Int> = emptySet(),
    46→    /** MRU filter chip IDs (up to 4), ordered most-recently-toggled first. */
    47→    val mruFilterIds: List<Int> = emptyList(),
    48→    val aperture: String? = null,
    49→    val shutterSpeed: String? = null,
    50→    val exposureCompensation: Float? = null,
    51→    val note: String = "",
    52→
    53→    val hasUnsavedChanges: Boolean = false,
    54→    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
    55→    val isSaving: Boolean = false,
    56→)
    57→
    58→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→// Events
    60→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→
    62→sealed class FrameDetailEvent {
    63→    data object SaveSuccessful : FrameDetailEvent()
    64→    /** User pressed back with unsaved changes. */
    65→    data object ConfirmDiscard : FrameDetailEvent()
    66→    /** GPS coordinates tapped — UI should open a geo Intent. */
    67→    data class OpenMapIntent(val lat: Double, val lng: Double) : FrameDetailEvent()
    68→}
    69→
    70→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    71→// ViewModel
    72→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73→
    74→class FrameDetailViewModel(
    75→    application: Application,
    76→    savedState: SavedStateHandle,
    77→) : AndroidViewModel(application) {
    78→
    79→    private val frameId: Int = checkNotNull(savedState["frameId"]) {
    80→        "FrameDetailViewModel requires a 'frameId' nav argument"
    81→    }
    82→
    83→    private val db = AppDatabase.getInstance(application)
    84→    private val frameRepository = FrameRepository(db)
    85→    private val rollRepository = RollRepository(db)
    86→    private val appPreferences = AppPreferences(application)
    87→
    88→    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(FrameDetailUiState())
    89→    val state: StateFlow<FrameDetailUiState> = _state.asStateFlow()
    90→
    91→    private val _events = Channel<FrameDetailEvent>(Channel.BUFFERED)
    92→    val events: Flow<FrameDetailEvent> = _events.receiveAsFlow()
    93→
    94→    // Track whether we've done the initial population so subsequent DB updates
    95→    // (e.g. another device, edge cases) don't overwrite the user's in-progress edits.
    96→    private var hasLoadedInitialData = false
    97→
    98→    init {
    99→        loadFrame()
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    private fun loadFrame() = viewModelScope.launch {
   103→        frameRepository.getFrameById(frameId).collect { frameWithDetails ->
   104→            if (!hasLoadedInitialData) {
   105→                hasLoadedInitialData = true
   106→                populateFromFrame(frameWithDetails)
   107→            }
   108→            // Always update the frame reference so lens/filter lists stay fresh
   109→            // (new lenses added to the roll would appear without re-launching).
   110→            _state.update { it.copy(frame = frameWithDetails) }
   111→        }
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    private suspend fun populateFromFrame(frameWithDetails: FrameWithDetails) {
   115→        val frame = frameWithDetails.frame
   116→        val roll = rollRepository.getRollById(frame.rollId).first()
   117→
   118→        // Build the lens list and shutter speed list from the roll's camera body
   119→        val rollLenses = roll.lenses.map { it.lens }
   120→        val cameraBody = null // We need to fetch the body from GearRepository
   121→        // The roll entity has cameraBodyId but not the full CameraBody object.
   122→        // RollWithDetails doesn't embed CameraBody — access via the roll's cameraBodyId.
   123→        // Load shutter speeds from the body via DB.
   124→        val db = AppDatabase.getInstance(getApplication())
   125→        val body = db.cameraBodyDao().getCameraBodyById(roll.roll.cameraBodyId).first()
   126→        val shutterSpeeds = ExposureValues.shutterSpeeds(body.shutterIncrements)
   127→        val rollFilters = roll.filters.map { it.filter }
   128→
   129→        // Determine available apertures for the initially selected lens
   130→        val selectedLens = rollLenses.firstOrNull { it.id == frame.lensId }
   131→        val apertures = selectedLens?.let {
   132→            ExposureValues.apertures(it.maxAperture, it.minAperture, it.apertureIncrements)
   133→        } ?: emptyList()
   134→
   135→        // Restore MRU filter list from SharedPreferences
   136→        val mruIds = appPreferences.getMruFilterIds(frame.rollId)
   137→            .filter { id -> rollFilters.any { it.id == id } }
   138→
   139→        val existingFilterIds = frameWithDetails.filters.map { it.id }.toSet()
   140→
   141→        _state.update {
   142→            it.copy(
   143→                frame = frameWithDetails,
   144→                rollLenses = rollLenses,
   145→                rollFilters = rollFilters,
   146→                availableShutterSpeeds = shutterSpeeds,
   147→                availableApertures = apertures,
   148→                isLogged = frame.isLogged,
   149→                selectedLensId = frame.lensId,
   150→                activeFilterIds = existingFilterIds,
   151→                mruFilterIds = mruIds,
   152→                aperture = frame.aperture,
   153→                shutterSpeed = frame.shutterSpeed,
   154→                exposureCompensation = frame.exposureCompensation,
   155→                note = frame.notes ?: "",
   156→                hasUnsavedChanges = false,
   157→                isLoading = false,
   158→            )
   159→        }
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   163→    // Field updates
   164→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   165→
   166→    fun onIsLoggedChanged(logged: Boolean) =
   167→        _state.update { it.copy(isLogged = logged, hasUnsavedChanges = true) }
   168→
   169→    fun onLensSelected(lensId: Int) {
   170→        val lens = _state.value.rollLenses.firstOrNull { it.id == lensId } ?: return
   171→        val apertures = ExposureValues.apertures(lens.maxAperture, lens.minAperture, lens.apertureIncrements)
   172→        // If current aperture is no longer valid for the new lens, snap to nearest available value
   173→        val currentAperture = _state.value.aperture
   174→        val adjustedAperture = if (currentAperture in apertures) currentAperture
   175→        else apertures.firstOrNull()
   176→
   177→        _state.update {
   178→            it.copy(
   179→                selectedLensId = lensId,
   180→                availableApertures = apertures,
   181→                aperture = adjustedAperture,
   182→                hasUnsavedChanges = true,
   183→            )
   184→        }
   185→    }
   186→
   187→    fun onFilterToggled(filterId: Int) {
   188→        val current = _state.value.activeFilterIds.toMutableSet()
   189→        if (filterId in current) current.remove(filterId) else current.add(filterId)
   190→
   191→        // Promote this filter to the front of the MRU list
   192→        appPreferences.promoteFilterInMru(_state.value.frame?.frame?.rollId ?: return, filterId)
   193→        val updatedMru = appPreferences.getMruFilterIds(_state.value.frame!!.frame.rollId)
   194→            .filter { id -> _state.value.rollFilters.any { it.id == id } }
   195→
   196→        _state.update {
   197→            it.copy(
   198→                activeFilterIds = current,
   199→                mruFilterIds = updatedMru,
   200→                hasUnsavedChanges = true,
   201→            )
   202→        }
   203→    }
   204→
   205→    fun onApertureChanged(aperture: String) =
   206→        _state.update { it.copy(aperture = aperture, hasUnsavedChanges = true) }
   207→
   208→    fun onShutterSpeedChanged(shutterSpeed: String) =
   209→        _state.update { it.copy(shutterSpeed = shutterSpeed, hasUnsavedChanges = true) }
   210→
   211→    fun onExposureCompensationChanged(ec: Float?) =
   212→        _state.update { it.copy(exposureCompensation = ec, hasUnsavedChanges = true) }
   213→
   214→    fun onNoteChanged(note: String) =
   215→        _state.update { it.copy(note = note, hasUnsavedChanges = true) }
   216→
   217→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   218→    // Save
   219→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   220→
   221→    fun onSaveTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   222→        val s = _state.value
   223→        val existingFrame = s.frame?.frame ?: return@launch
   224→
   225→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = true) }
   226→
   227→        val updatedFrame = Frame(
   228→            id = existingFrame.id,
   229→            rollId = existingFrame.rollId,
   230→            frameNumber = existingFrame.frameNumber, // immutable
   231→            isLogged = s.isLogged,
   232→            // Preserve loggedAt: if newly marking as logged and no timestamp exists, set now.
   233→            // If unmarking as logged, clear the timestamp.
   234→            // If was already logged, keep original timestamp (retroactive correction).
   235→            loggedAt = when {
   236→                !s.isLogged -> null
   237→                existingFrame.loggedAt != null -> existingFrame.loggedAt
   238→                else -> System.currentTimeMillis()
   239→            },
   240→            aperture = s.aperture,
   241→            shutterSpeed = s.shutterSpeed,
   242→            lensId = s.selectedLensId,
   243→            exposureCompensation = s.exposureCompensation,
   244→            lat = existingFrame.lat, // GPS not editable on Frame Detail — captured on log
   245→            lng = existingFrame.lng,
   246→            notes = s.note.trim().ifBlank { null },
   247→        )
   248→
   249→        // Compute filter delta: add new, remove de-selected
   250→        val previousFilterIds = s.frame.filters.map { it.id }.toSet()
   251→        val currentFilterIds = s.activeFilterIds
   252→
   253→        val filtersToAdd = (currentFilterIds - previousFilterIds).map { filterId ->
   254→            FrameFilter(frameId = existingFrame.id, filterId = filterId)
   255→        }
   256→        val filterIdsToRemove = (previousFilterIds - currentFilterIds).toList()
   257→
   258→        frameRepository.logFrame(updatedFrame, filtersToAdd, filterIdsToRemove)
   259→
   260→        _state.update { it.copy(isSaving = false, hasUnsavedChanges = false) }
   261→        _events.send(FrameDetailEvent.SaveSuccessful)
   262→    }
   263→
   264→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   265→    // Navigation
   266→    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   267→
   268→    fun onBackPressed() = viewModelScope.launch {
   269→        if (_state.value.hasUnsavedChanges) {
   270→            _events.send(FrameDetailEvent.ConfirmDiscard)
   271→        }
   272→    }
   273→
   274→    fun onGpsCoordinatesTapped() = viewModelScope.launch {
   275→        val frame = _state.value.frame?.frame ?: return@launch
   276→        val lat = frame.lat ?: return@launch
   277→        val lng = frame.lng ?: return@launch
   278→        _events.send(FrameDetailEvent.OpenMapIntent(lat, lng))
   279→    }
   280→}

package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.relation

import androidx.room.Embedded
import androidx.room.Junction
import androidx.room.Relation
import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Filter
import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Frame
import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.FrameFilter
import com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity.Lens

/**
 * A Frame with its active Lens and all active Filters at the time of exposure.
 *
 * [lens] is null when the frame is unlogged (Frame.lensId is null).
 * [filters] is empty when no filters were active.
 *
 * Queries returning this type must be annotated with @Transaction.
 */
data class FrameWithDetails(
    @Embedded val frame: Frame,

    /** The lens used for this exposure. Null if the frame is unlogged. */
    @Relation(parentColumn = "lensId", entityColumn = "id")
    val lens: Lens? = null,

    /** Filters active at the time of this exposure, via the FrameFilter join table. */
    @Relation(
        parentColumn = "id",
        entityColumn = "id",
        associateBy = Junction(
            value = FrameFilter::class,
            parentColumn = "frameId",
            entityColumn = "filterId",
        ),
    )
    val filters: List<Filter> = emptyList(),
)

package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity

import androidx.room.Entity
import androidx.room.PrimaryKey

/**
 * A filter in the gear library.
 *
 * [filterType] is user-defined vocabulary (folksonomy). Suggested starting values: color, ND,
 * polarizer, graduated ND, UV, infrared — but not enforced. UI uses autocomplete with existing
 * values plus "add new" option.
 *
 * [evReduction] is nullable because some filters (UV, skylight 1A) have no meaningful exposure
 * reduction. When displaying the filter EV sum, only non-null values are summed. A "~" prefix
 * is shown if any active filter has null evReduction.
 *
 * [filterSizeMm] null means the filter works with any lens (slot filters, gel filters, etc.).
 * Null-size filters always appear in filter pickers regardless of lens thread size.
 *
 * Filters stack — a frame can have multiple active filters via the FrameFilter join table.
 */
@Entity(tableName = "filters")
data class Filter(
    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true) val id: Int = 0,
    val name: String,
    val make: String,
    val filterType: String,
    /** Stops of light reduction. Null for filters with no meaningful exposure effect. */
    val evReduction: Float? = null,
    val filterSizeMm: Int? = null,
    val notes: String? = null,
)

Solved using `navController.getBackStackEntry(RollSetup::class)` (Navigation 2.8.0 type-safe back stack lookup) to get a `RollSetupViewModel` instance scoped to the RollSetup back stack entry. KitSelectorScreen then calls `rollSetupViewModel.onKitSelected(kit)` directly before popping.
   - **EC null state**: ViewModel has `exposureCompensation: Float?`. Since there's no explicit "no EC" in the stepper list, null is treated as pointing to the 0.0 EV index. Tapping the value display triggers `onExposureCompensationChanged(null)` to clear.
   - **Aperture stepper direction**: List is widest-first, so `+` (more light/wider) decrements the index, `−` (less light/narrower) increments the index.
